location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall

description: This session will provide instructors with some practical insight on how to best serve first generation students enrolled in their classes. We will cover general student preparedness; advice on small changes in syllabus supplementation, curriculum design and/or delivery; and other factors that impact first generation students at UNLV.

name: Graduate Student Open House
location:
Lied Library
        Lied Graduate Commons (Room 2141, Second Floor)
description: The Graduate & Professional Student Association (GPSA) and the Graduate College will host an open house. You can drop in between 10 a.m. and 4 p.m. to spin for swag, enjoy some tasty treats, and connect with fellow graduate and professional students.

name: Building Habits That Support A Growth Mindset
description: A growth mindset is something you practice, not just believe. The gap between knowing and doing is where habits connect values to action. This session helps you turn what you believe into what you consistently do by linking your values to simple, repeatable habits that drive growth. We use a growth mindset lens to turn values into habits and show how aspirational values keep you open to learning. Participants will leave with clear, doable steps they can use at work and in life.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: 2001 Retro Day: Lied Library Spirit Week
description: Step back to 2001 and help us kick off Lied Library Spirit Week with Retro Day! Celebrate the year Lied Library opened by enjoying movies and music straight from the early 2000s, alongside a display of vintage items that capture the look, feel, and pop culture of the era. Stop by to relive the moment, grab a snack or refreshment, and start spirit week with a little nostalgia.

name: Lied Library Video Studio Orientation
description: Discover the essentials of video production in this hands-on orientation with our library multimedia team. Step into our state-of-the-art Video Production Studio and learn how to bring your creative projects to life. In just 30 minutes, you'll get practical experience with:

name: Virtual: Grad 201 Admissions - Criteria, Context, and Considerations
description: Need help with your grad application? Join us for a workshop to assist with your graduate application. Join us for a workshop on common elements of graduate school applications. In this information session, we will:

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Admissions Information Session
description: These virtual information sessions are for individuals who are starting to consider medical school as a graduate degree option and career path. We welcome high school seniors, community college and university students, or other individuals just beginning to research medical school and/or beginning to plan for their application. We will offer an overview of the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV, resources to help consider medical school, details regarding application, and a Q&A at the end of the session.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Beyond the Gold Standard: Rethinking Evidence in Health Research
location:
Lied Library
        Rhyolite Room or Online
description: This workshop explores the origins and limitations of traditional evidence-based models in health research and examines how these frameworks can privilege certain methodologies and forms of knowledge. Participants will compare dominant evidence hierarchies with alternative approaches to assessing rigor and relevance, and consider how expanding definitions of evidence can support more inclusive, interdisciplinary, and impactful health research. This workshop is part of the Rebel+ Libraries Workshop Series.

name: Fraternity & Sorority Life Meet and Greet
description: Warm up your spring semester at the Fraternity & Sorority Life Meet and Greet with free donuts! Connect with Fraternity & Sorority Life, meet new friends, and start the semester on a sweet note.

name: Discussion: PTI Teaching and Resource Hub
description: Please join our online discussion to learn about how the new PTI online hub can support your teaching and learning goals, meet other PTI colleagues, and discover opportunities at UNLV's Teaching and Learning Commons.

name: BSN Program Virtual Information Session
description: UNLV Nursing welcomes students interested in pursuing a career in nursing to join a virtual informational session on January 21 from 11 a.m. to 12 p.m. to learn more about the pre-licensure program. This session will outline the pathway from pre-requisite work to admission to the School of Nursing program, provide an overview of the program, courses, clinical rotations, and resources, and include support from student services, the Student Nurse Association, and more. Prospective students will also have the opportunity to ask questions directly to the program directors.

name: PAWsitive Start: Dog Therapy
description: Join the University Libraries for a de-stress session with our furry dog friends from Pet Partners as you embark on your spring semester! This event is part of Lied Library Spirit Week.

name: Lied Is My Second Home Day: Lied Library Spirit Week
description: If Lied Library feels like your go-to spot between classes, late-night study sessions, or just a place to recharge, this day is for you. Lied Is My Second Home Day is all about celebrating the cozy, familiar, and favorite parts of Lied. Swing by the lobby for comforting treats, share what you love most about the library, and jump into fun, feel-good activities that capture why Lied really does feel like a second home. Come for the snacks, stay for the vibes and the memories.

name: Lied Library Video Studio Orientation
description: Discover the essentials of video production in this hands-on orientation with our library multimedia team. Step into our state-of-the-art Video Production Studio and learn how to bring your creative projects to life. In just 30 minutes, you'll get practical experience with:

description: Chelsea Wheeler-Mayo, Ph.D Candidate Department of Criminal Justice A Feminist-Foucauldian Narrative Analysis of Institutional and Cultural Betrayal in Military Sexual Misconduct Advisory Committee Members: Emily Troshynski, Advisory Committee Chair Terance Miethe, Advisory Committee Member Hong Lu, Advisory Committee Member Nicholas Barr, Graduate College Representative

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: REB Spring Welcome Back Event: Rebel Grand Prix
description: Come celebrate the spring semester with REB at this event with free food, activities, and more!

name: 10 Strategies for Improving Your Finances
description: The current economic climate brings both challenges and opportunities from a financial perspective. You may wonder during these challenging times: “What should I be most careful about” and “What should my family and I work towards?” The 10 financial tips discussed in this workshop will get you on the road to financial well-being during challenging times.

name: Virtual Graduate: Healthcare Administration and Public Health Info. Session
description: Considering a degree in healthcare administration or public health? Join our information session today to find out more! We will discuss the benefits of graduate study in the UNLV School of Public Health, as well as the differences between the master of healthcare administration, the executive master of healthcare administration, and the master of public health.

name: Lied Library Birthday Bash: Lied Library Spirit Week
location:
Lied Library
        Amargosa Room, Rhyolite Room, Lobby
description: Lied Library is turning 25, and we’re throwing a party to celebrate! Join us for the Lied Library Birthday Bash, a high-energy celebration packed with treats, snacks, refreshments, karaoke, lotería, and plenty of fun activities to mark 25 years of learning, community, and memories. Whether you’re belting out a song, jumping into a game, or just stopping by for something sweet, this is your chance to celebrate a library that’s been at the heart of campus for a quarter century. Come for the fun, stay for the treats, and help us celebrate 25 years of Lied Library.

name: Reframing Diversity: What it Means at UNLV in 2026
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 240 and Online.
description: Reyes J. Luna, Director, Student Leadership, Identity, & Community Engagement in Student Affairs and team will lead an information session on campus for faculty to explore DEI re-organization, calendar of upcoming events, service learning, and community partnerships.

name: Becoming an Adaptive Perfectionist
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 1502 (Health Education Room)
description: Learn how to rethink the relationship between motivation and self-criticism, set manageable goals, and be more productive while also tending to your wellbeing.

name: National Pie Day (Pilates Event)
description: Celebrate National Pie Day with us! Join us for a special pilates class, then enjoy a personal pie and latte!

name: 2026 Vallen Dinner of Distinction
description: Join the Harrah College of Hospitality for an extraordinary evening celebrating excellence in hospitality: The 2026 Vallen Dinner of Distinction. Rather than a traditional gala, this year's Vallen Dinner is a luxury lounge-style event with chef tasting stations, elevated beverage activations, immersive entertainment, and exclusive suite-style seating. The evening is an opportunity to honor the leaders who are shaping the global hospitality industry and recognize the college for ushering in a new and innovative era in hospitality education.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Guided Breathing Practice
description: Revitalize your mind and body in just 15 minutes with alternate nostril breathing. This time-honored technique from Northern India is designed to bring your nervous system into balance, helping you maintain a steady, focused energy throughout the day. Studies show it can help lower blood pressure, improve lung capacity, and support circulation. Whether you need an energy boost or a grounding practice to center yourself, this session has you covered.

name: UNLV Geographic Information Systems (GIS) Bootcamp
description: Join the University Libraries for the 2026 UNLV Geographic Information Systems (GIS) bootcamp. This program takes place across two Fridays and is designed for UNLV faculty interested in integrating GIS into their research. You will strengthen your spatial thinking, learn spatial analysis tools, and practice producing high-quality, publication-ready maps. By the end of this program, you will be familiar with the following topics:

name: UNLV GIS Bootcamp 2026
description: Join the University Libraries for the 2026 UNLV GIS Bootcamp. This program takes place across two Fridays and is designed for UNLV faculty interested in integrating geographic information systems (GIS) into their research. By the end of this program, you will be familiar with the following topics: GIS Data Management Spatial Data Cleaning & Analysis Spatial Statistics Programming with Spatial Data Lunch will be provided.

name: APIME-FSA: Institute for Financial Literacy & Wellness Presentation
description: APIME-FSA members are invited to a tour and presentation by John Hirano of the UNLV Institute for Financial Literacy & Wellness. Learn what programming and services are available to faculty/staff as well as students.

name: First Gen Welcome Back Social
description: Stop by The Intersection to network with other first generation students, faculty and staff. Make sure to pick up a calendar of events for the semester. We look forward to seeing you there!

name: Getting Started Online: Skills, Tips & Strategies
description: Kick off the semester with practical tips for succeeding in courses with online components. This interactive session includes engaging activities and helpful strategies for managing online coursework, staying organized, and using digital tools effectively. Open to all students.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Faculty Senate General Education Spring 2026 Meeting 1
description: Regular monthly meeting of the Faculty Senate General Education Committee.

1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

UNLV Alumni Night at Rebel Hockey

Early Outreach Day Camp - From Strip to Stage: A Las Vegas Story
description: Join us for a fun and engaging day learning about the city we call home. Students will discover how Las Vegas has transformed from a gaming-focused city to a world-famous center for entertainment, sports, and the arts!

name: Camp Cosmos Jr.
description: Camp Cosmos Jr. is an astronomy day camp for upper elementary school students in grades 3-5. This day camp will feature lessons and activities about stars, planets, galaxies, and the universe. Students will engage in hands-on learning experiences and form new friendships.

name: UNLV College of Education Student Mixer
description: College of Education undergraduate students are invited to attend a student mixer as the spring semester kicks off. Come by for free food and door prizes, meet other education and human services students, get involved with a COE student organization or two, and learn about the services available to students. This community-building event is one you don’t want to miss! This event is hosted by your College of Education CSUN Senator, Melvin Taylor.

name: Gratitude Brief Practice
description: Research has shown practicing gratitude leads to a number of mental, physical, and relational benefits; yet it is so easy to be swept up in the seemingly relentless demands of a busy life and feel you don’t have the time to take a moment to consider and appreciate the things you are grateful for. Give yourself the gift of this 10-minute gratitude practice and prioritize your self-care and well-being. Register here for our 10 to 15-minute gratitude practice!

The MSW graduate program informational sessions are designed to provide prospective applicants with valuable information about the social work graduate program. These sessions will thoroughly review the application requirements and offer applicants an opportunity to ask questions they may have about the program and the application process.

description: We will cover topics such as goal setting, organization tips, and campus resources. We will also discuss the wide range of services and facilities available to students, including academic support centers, career services, health and wellness resources, and student organizations. Students will receive a Google invite from SASCoaching@unlv.edu with further information.

description: College of Education undergraduate students are invited to attend a student mixer as the spring semester kicks off. Come by for free food and door prizes, meet other education and human services students, get involved with a COE student organization or two, and learn about the services available to students. This community-building event is one you don’t want to miss! This event is hosted by your College of Education CSUN Senator, Melvin Taylor.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Guided Imagery Practice
description: The mind is a powerful tool! Join us in this brief relaxation exercise as we focus our minds, using our imagination. No experience is necessary. Participants will be briefly educated on what guided imagery is then led through a series of verbal cues and suggestions into their own private place of rest and restoration. Approximately 15 minutes total.

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 235 (Faculty Lounge)
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Podcasting 101: Basics of Recording & Editing
description: Join David Ramos Candelas, Multimedia and Design Specialist, to learn the basics of podcast recording and editing using technology found inside of Lied Library's Multimedia Production Studios. During this one-hour workshop, you will explore Lied Library's multimedia resources and practice basic recording and editing.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

description: Learn how to choose a graduate school program that is right for you! Hear from admission reviewers on what stands out to them when they are reviewing applications. Get advice on admission requirements and what to expect when you are applying to different schools and programs. By participating in this workshop, you will:

name: Administrative Faculty Committee January Meeting
description: Regular monthly meeting to discuss issues of importance to administrative faculty. All administrative faculty are invited to attend in person or online via Zoom. They can also submit items for the agenda anonymously via our New Business for AFC form.

name: Spring Career Kick-Off
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Interior Courtyard
description: Join Lee's Career and Professional Development Team at our Fall Career Kick-Off event! All Lee students are invited to learn about our services, and pick up information about how to get the job you want when you graduate. Attending students will be able to learn more about our career resources, including Handshake and Vmock. In addition, students can visit the Troesh Center Career Closet if they have a need for professional clothing (free of charge).

name: Resistance Band Workshop
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        SRWC MAC Court 1
description: Curious about how a resistance band can prepare you for a great workout? Come on down to the Student Rec and Wellness Center, where you can learn some of the benefits of using a resistance band as well exercises to target specific areas of the body.

name: International Holocaust Remembrance Day Commemoration
description: We invite the UNLV community to gather in remembrance, reflection and responsibility to keep the memory alive of those lost. The theme of this year's commemoration centers on why we continue to remember the Holocaust today. Remembrance today is not optional -- it is urgent.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: Digital Vision Board Workshop
description: Take time to reflect, reset, and look ahead by creating a digital vision board for the semester. In this guided workshop, students will explore their academic, personal, and career goals and translate them into a visual representation using free digital tools like Google Slides or Canva. Participants will leave with a personalized vision board they can save as a phone or desktop background, designed to help keep goals visible, motivating, and top of mind throughout the semester.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
2026 Interdisciplinary Research Symposium
location:
Student Union
        Ballroom A, B, and C
description: Collaborate. Innovate. Accelerate. The UNLV Interdisciplinary Research Symposium brings together faculty, staff, and students from across the university to explore the power of collaboration. Join the Division of Research for a day of interactive sessions, lightning talks, and networking designed to:

name: Essential Tech Skills for Online Coursework
description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. This session will cover essential tech skills students need to succeed in online classes but are often expected to already know.

name: Spring 2026 Involvement Fair
description: The Involvement Fair is an opportunity for UNLV students to learn about UNLV Registered Student Organizations (RSOs), departments/offices, and non-profit community organizations in the Las Vegas area. The Involvement Fair is also an opportunity for RSOs to meet new students, collaborate with other organizations, and network with partners in the community. Existing and prospective student organizations, university departments, and invited non-profit community organizations are welcome to participate.

name: Spring into Spring Semester Social
description: All UNLV students are invited to join the Libraries for our Spring Into Spring Semester social! Drop-in anytime during the event, meet other students, and mix and mingle with library faculty, staff, and other campus experts. New to campus? Get to know the specifics of UNLV and start building your community of support. Returning to campus? Connect, play games, and network with your peers. Enter to win RebelCash and other prizes. Refreshments and snacks provided.

name: UNLV Women's Council Vision Board Event
description: The Women’s Council is hosting a vision board event. Plan your 2026 goals and dreams in this fun and interactive event. All materials will be provided, including magazines, scissors, glue, and poster boards. Please feel free to bring any personal images that resonate with you. No artistic skills required!

name: Money Talks – The Student Finance Playbook: Maximize Funding for Next Semester
location:
Student Services Complex - Building C
        Institute for Financial Literacy and Wellness
description: Join UNLV’s Institute for Financial Literacy and Wellness (IFLW) and the Office of Financial Aid & Scholarships on Wednesday, January 28, from 12 - 1 p.m. to learn actionable steps for securing your tuition funds. During the Student Finance Playbook: Maximize Your Funding for Next Semester workshop, we'll show you how to successfully maintain your current aid, uncover hidden scholarships and grants, and implement effective strategies for managing your existing financial resources.

name: Keep Calm and Carry Yarn
description: Yarn crafters and yarn-craft-curious folks are invited to join a Keep Calm and Carry Yarn gathering in the Lied Library lobby. We invite thread and embroidery floss crafters to join us! Bring your current work in progress, or stop by for inspiration and conversation. The Libraries' Makerspace will provide material samples and other supplies. Completing a makerspace orientation is not required for these events.

name: Recovery Curious
location:
Student Services Complex - Building A
        CAPS Suite 143
description: Are you curious about substance use recovery or ways to reduce use? Join us virtually or in-person to share questions or receive support around the topic of substance use and recovery.

name: Success Series: Time Management #1
description: This workshop will help students build effective academic habits to support success throughout the semester. We will discuss motivation, goal-setting, and planning strategies to help avoid procrastination and manage time more effectively. Whether you’re a busy student juggling multiple responsibilities or simply looking to improve your productivity, this session is for you. The UNLV Writing Center will also join us to share strategies for building healthy writing habits, including making time for writing and staying consistent with assignments.

name: New Employee & Benefits Orientation
description: The university provides a competitive benefits package to benefit eligible employees. During new employee and benefits orientation, we provide an introduction to UNLV and important information about its history and culture; policies and procedures; services and resources available to employees; workplace protection; health, retirement, and voluntary benefits; and more.

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. This session will cover essential tech skills students need to succeed in online classes but are often expected to already know.

name: It's Just Coffee: Public and Private Education
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups (approximately once a month) for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 1502 (Health Education Room)
description: Learn how to rethink the relationship between motivation and self-criticism, set manageable goals, and be more productive while also tending to your wellbeing.

name: Graduate College: Degrees of Pressure: Navigating Anxiety and Stress in Academia
description: Graduate school can be a thrilling period of intellectual growth, but for many students, it is also marked by substantial anxiety, stress, and burnout. Graduate students report significantly higher levels of anxiety than the general population, and poor mental well-being is a leading contributor to attrition from graduate programs—both heartbreaking and costly for students and faculty alike.

name: FYE -  Campus Kick Off: Spring Edition
description: Join us for the first FYE milestone event for spring, where we will welcome our new and returning students and celebrate academic achievement! There will be food, activities, and mingling. The FYE Connection Award winners will also be recognized.

name: Global Thursday: Culture and Conversation
description: Learn about different cultures and share your own, enjoy international snacks, and make new friends every Global Thursday. All UNLV students are welcome!

name: Clinical Trials: History, Oversight, and UNLV - Virtual Research Seminar
description: The Office of Research invites you to our upcoming virtual research seminar. Faculty, residents, fellows, and students across all health sciences are invited to attend. Learning Objectives Differentiate the phases of clinical trials and their relevance to patient care and clinical decision-making. Describe the onboarding process for clinical trials at UNLV, including clinician roles and regulatory requirements. Identify institutional resources that are established to support clinicians serving as Principal Investigators (PIs).

name: 2026 Memorial Beecroft Lecture
description: Marks your calendars for Thursday, January 29, 2026, as the annual Beecroft Lecture welcomes Jennifer Lackey to present her talk, "Epistemic Reparations and the Right be Known in our Criminal Legal System." When victims of gross human rights violations and systemic injustice are also victims of false or partial narratives, reparations must include epistemic reparations. In such cases, people have a right to be known, to be witnessed, and to participate in the truth telling of what occurred.

name: A Mentally Healthy New Year
description: Start the new year with clarity, connection, and practical tools for mental well-being. This event brings together two expert speakers to help you understand mental health across the lifespan, recognize when support may be needed, and learn effective ways to calm the mind and body. Through real-world examples and guided discussion, this event helps participants feel more confident recognizing what is part of normal aging, what may be treatable, and how to speak openly about their well-being without fear or shame.

name: President’s Investment Challenge – 2026 Launch & Orientation Session
description: The President’s Investment Challenge officially begins with an Investment Challenge Orientation. Teams will receive an overview of the competition format, scoring criteria, and key market dynamics. Each team will also receive initial virtual capital to start building their portfolios. Not Sure if You Want To Compete? If you haven’t signed up yet, feel free to attend the session to learn more and decide whether to join the challenge!

No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: UNLV Global Success Series: Time Management
description: New international students feel overwhelmed when they arrive in the US, adjusting to a new unfamiliar school and cultural environment. They are not used to taking multiple tests in courses, doing group projects, or having many papers due. If you feel overwhelmed and need to manage your time better, this is the session for you! Join Academic Success Coach Sydnee Han for this informative presentation.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Building Tour - Kerkorian Medical Education Building
description: In 2022, the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV opened its new educational facility in the Las Vegas Medical District. The Office of Admissions is now offering a building tour program for those considering medical education (MD) degree studies. MEB tours are led by student ambassadors and will go over very general information prior to the tour. For more detailed admissions information, attendees will be provided materials and encouraged to sign up for a virtual information session, which is offered monthly.

name: "Abortion and Utility"
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room C122
description: Alastair Norcross, Dept. of Philosophy, University of Colorado, Boulder—In this talk, I explore what position a utilitarian should take on (i) the morality and (ii) the legality of abortion. Regarding (i), since utilitarians, like other consequentialists, deny any fundamental moral significance to the doing/allowing distinction, abortion would appear to have roughly the same moral status as the (successful) use of contraception or the choice not to procreate. I present and criticize the case for saying that utilitarians should be in favor of increased human procreation.

name: BMI Live: The Art of Retelling the Truth featuring Melissa Febos, Jamie Hood, and Isle McElroy
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Recital Hall (BMC 115A)
description: Melissa Febos and Jamie Hood are two of the most astute essayists working today on the subject of trauma and embodiment.

Fees apply) For more information, visit the UNLV Newman Center website.

Gear up for the BEST Career Expo with our comprehensive workshop series designed to equip students with the essential tools to connect with employers and secure your dream job. Each workshop provides you an opportunity to gain invaluable insights from industry experts and employers who will guide you through key aspects of career preparation. All UNLV students are invited to participate.

name: Ed.D. in Educational Leadership Information Session
description: Join us for a virtual information session on Nevada’s premiere doctoral program for educational leaders. The 60 credit hour Doctorate in Educational Leadership (Ed.D.) degree aims to help leaders grow in visionary thinking and develop innovative solutions to current and future educational challenges.

During this class we will guide you through different exercises, techniques and strategies for preparing for the swimming leg of a triathlon. We invite anyone regardless of swimming level or triathlon experience to join us in this free event.

UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Slices of Wisdom - Pizza & Conversation with the Dean of the Graduate College
location:
University Gateway Building
        2nd Floor, Gather Conference Room
description: Join us for Slices of Wisdom, a casual and engaging event where graduate students can enjoy free pizza and connect directly with the dean of the Graduate College. This is your chance to: Ask questions about grad life, academics, or career paths Share your experiences and ideas Get to know the dean and fellow graduate students Enjoy some good food and great conversation Whether you have something on your mind or just want to grab a slice and say hi, we’d love to see you there!

name: Success Series: Building Your Blueprint
description: We’ll cover the basics of setting effective goals, share helpful tools and resources available at UNLV, and leave time for questions and reflection. The UNLV Writing Center will also join us to discuss writing habits and organizational strategies, helping students plan writing tasks, manage assignments, and stay organized throughout the semester. By the end of the session, students will walk away with a clearer vision and a personalized blueprint to guide their semester.

name: Strategic Searching with ProQuest's AI Research Assistant: A Critical, Hands-On Workshop
description: This workshop introduces participants to the ProQuest AI Research Assistant, an embedded, discipline-aware generative AI tool designed to strengthen search strategies, accelerate document evaluation, and support deeper engagement with scholarly literature. Through live demonstrations and hands-on practice, participants will learn to use features such as key takeaways, findings, essential details, and topic visualizations, while developing strategies to critically evaluate and verify AI-generated outputs.

name: REBELPerform Evaluation Training
description: All administrative and classified staff complete their performance evaluations on REBELPerform. This training will provide an overview of the REBELPerform evaluation process. You will learn how to access the evaluation, navigate through the form, add attachments to the evaluation, download evaluations as a PDF, print the evaluations, and more. There will also be a live demonstration and an opportunity to ask questions. This training also serves as a refresher course for those who need additional guidance on how to complete the performance evaluation.

It will also review our student-facing modules on AI literacy and ethical use available for all UNLV instructors on WebCampus. The session will consider how these supports can be integrated into teaching and learning.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center

description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

description: Join David Ramos Candelas, multimedia and design specialist, to learn the basics of podcast recording and editing using technology found inside of Lied Library's Multimedia Production Studios. Kim Groberg of High Style for a Hundred brings her years of Corporate HR experience along with her luxury retail executive experience to help you learn how to take your wardrobe from basic to WOW without spending a dime.

name: Writing Essentials Wednesdays
description: This semester, join the Writing Center on the first Wednesday of each month at 1 p.m. to strengthen your writing habits and skills. This spring, we'll be focusing on starting out strong. Have a paper due soon that you haven't yet begun? Were you just assigned an essay for class? Attend this workshop to learn strategies to set yourself up for success. We'll touch on topics such as turning your assignment prompt into a helpful checklist; organizing your ideas; and citing your sources.

Whether you're exploring career options or preparing for your next step, this panel will help you identify and articulate the strengths that set you apart.

name: Graduate: Virtual MBA Program Overview: Online vs. Hybrid Evening Options
description: In this session, we provide a comprehensive look at the UNLV Lee Business School MBA programs, its structure, benefits, and what sets it apart. Join us as we compare the online and hybrid evening options. Discover the key features, benefits, and differences between these flexible learning formats to help you decide which option best suits your professional and personal needs. Gain insights into course structure, scheduling, and support services available for both formats.

Whether you’re commuting on two wheels or carrying valuable tech, we welcome students, faculty, and staff to register bicycles, scooters, laptops, tablets, phones, and more. Registering your property helps us assist in recovering your belongings in case of theft or loss. To make your visit quick and easy, please bring the following information for each item you’d like to register: Serial number Model number

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Room 112
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: Community of Practice: Effectively Managing Undergraduate Classroom Writing
description: The UNLV Writing Center, in partnership with the Teaching & Learning Commons, offers this Community of Practice with the aim of bringing together instructors on campus to discuss and brainstorm best practices in teaching writing.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Communicating Without Conflict With Your Significant Other
description: Communication is the cornerstone of all relationships. It reinforces trust and acts as a method by which you can share your problems, frustrations, anxieties, hopes and successes. Conflict is a part of life and exists in relationships. In fact, a relationship with no apparent conflict may be unhealthier than one with frequent conflict. Conflicts are critical events that can weaken or strengthen a relationship.

name: Webinar: I Thought I Could Retire by Now: Managing the Emotions of Delayed Retirement
description: In this webinar, we will focus on the emotional experience of realizing you may need to work longer than you originally anticipated. Participants will explore the feelings that can arise in this situation, including disappointment, stress, and uncertainty, and how these emotions can impact overall well-being. This webinar will consider the pros and cons of delaying retirement and examine the emotional differences between involuntary and voluntary retirement. We will touch on common life circumstances and broader societal forces that contribute to extended working years.

name: Success Series: Time Management #2
description: This workshop will help students build effective academic habits to support success throughout the semester. We will discuss motivation, goal-setting, and planning strategies to help avoid procrastination and manage time more effectively. Whether you’re a busy student juggling multiple responsibilities or simply looking to improve your productivity, this session is for you. The UNLV Writing Center will also join us to share strategies for building healthy writing habits, including making time for writing and staying consistent with assignments.

name: Becoming an Adaptive Perfectionist
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 1502 (Health Education Room)
description: Learn how to rethink the relationship between motivation and self-criticism, set manageable goals, and be more productive while also tending to your wellbeing.

name: Global Thursday: Language Exchange
description: Interested in learning or practicing a new language? Do you love exploring language-culture connections? Join us every second Global Thursday for the Language Exchange Program (LEP) where students can explore languages such as Chinese, German, Vietnamese, Persian, Japanese, and more in a relaxed environment. No prior study required!

location:
Richard Tam Alumni Center

description: Kick off Black History Month with an evening of celebration, connection, and community. This event features a resource fair, live DJ, soul food, and a 106 & Park–inspired theme. Attendees are encouraged to dress as their favorite early 2000s icon and participate in the Best Dressed Icon contest. Open to everyone. More details and RSVP

Active Assailant 8-Hour Hands-On Training

description: Join the University Police Department for an 8-hour, hands-on Active Assailant Training designed to help participants develop practical response strategies and confidence in high-stress situations. This interactive, scenario-based course focuses on preparedness, situational awareness, and coordinated response techniques.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Cure 4 The Kids
description: About the Site Cure 4 The Kids is a nonprofit pediatric specialty clinic dedicated to providing comprehensive medical care to children with chronic and life-threatening illnesses. The clinic supports patients and families through compassionate, high-quality care and a child-centered environment.

name: Project 150 Service Day
description: About the Site Project 150 is a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting homeless, displaced, and disadvantaged high school students in Southern Nevada. The organization provides free resources such as food, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and other essential support to help students stay in school and work toward graduation in a stable and dignified way.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Center for Urban Water Conservation
description: About the Site: The UNLV Center for Urban Water Conservation is an outdoor facility dedicated to promoting sustainable water use in the Las Vegas Valley through education, research, and community engagement. The center plays a key role in conservation efforts by supporting environmentally responsible practices and hands-on community learning. Service Activities:

name: Lake Mead Service Day
description: About the Site Lake Mead National Recreation Area is one of the most visited national parks in the United States and plays a vital role in environmental conservation, water sustainability, and outdoor recreation in Southern Nevada. The site provides critical natural habitats, recreational access, and educational opportunities while facing ongoing environmental challenges such as litter, erosion, and water conservation concerns.

name: Project Marilyn Service Day
description: About the Site Project Marilyn is a nonprofit organization in Southern Nevada dedicated to supporting individuals experiencing homelessness by providing access to essential resources, advocacy, and community-based support. The organization focuses on restoring dignity and stability by addressing immediate needs while connecting individuals to longer-term support services.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Three Square Food Bank
description: About the Site Three Square Food Bank is Southern Nevada’s only food bank and a vital resource in the fight against hunger in our community. Through food distribution, community partnerships, and advocacy, Three Square works to ensure that individuals and families across the region have consistent access to nutritious food.

name: Opportunity Village Service Day
description: About the Site Opportunity Village is a nonprofit organization that serves individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities in Southern Nevada. Through employment training, day programs, and community-based services, Opportunity Village empowers participants to build independence, develop job skills, and engage meaningfully with their community.

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV 10th Annual Pre-Medicine Conference
description: The Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV Office of Community Affairs is pleased to present the 10th Annual Pre-Medicine Conference for first and second-year pre-medicine students at Nevada System of Higher Education (NSHE) institutions.

name: HSI Resource Hub Webinar: Transforming STEM Education at HSIs: Evidence-Based, Community-Engaged Learning for High-Impact Student Success
description: Led by Yufeng Wei, Wei Zhang, Jennifer Pax, and Allison Fitzgerald from New Jersey City University, this webinar explores how Community-Engaged Learning (CEL) is transforming STEM education at New Jersey City University (NJCU), one of the oldest Hispanic-Serving Institutions (HSIs) in the State of New Jersey, by leveraging evidence-based, high-impact practices. NJCU’s CEL Framework is defined as a holistic, reciprocal partnership between students, faculty, and community organizations, integrating academic learning with meaningful, real-world experiences.

name: New Faculty Academy: Cultivating Your Network of Mentors and Collaborators
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        101
description: Academic faculty members who are new to UNLV, as well as those who want an inspirational boost, are invited to a panel discussion: Cultivating Your Network of Mentors and Collaborators. A panel of current faculty will discuss the importance of building a diverse network of mentors and collaborators who can provide guidance, feedback, and opportunities throughout your career. Panelists will share strategies for identifying potential mentors, fostering meaningful collaborations, and leveraging both formal and informal networks to support professional growth and success.

name: Study Abroad Scholarships: Part 1 - Freeman Asia & Gilman Information Session
description: Learn how to apply for the Gilman and Freeman-ASIA scholarships—both need-based and perfect for study abroad students; we'll cover eligibility, deadlines, and help review your essays and applications! It’s an easy way to boost your chances for free money to go abroad!

name: Depression 101: Mood Boosting Tips and Tricks
description: Learn about depression and practical strategies to improve your mood!

name: Shop Talk: The Word Makes the World: Reclaiming Language and Creating Imagined Histories
description: In her book The Volcano Daughters, Gina María Balibrera’s work creates a textured and nuanced landscape of 1932 El Salvador. There’s a sense of reclamation within the novel, whether it be Salvadoran legends that are subverted, or the use of Spanish language (specifically Salvadoran unique words) unapologetically throughout the novel.

name: Graduate MENTORS: Mid-Program Check-In
description: The Graduate College is excited to invite all Graduate MENTORS participants to come together for our mid-program check-in meeting! This informal and supportive meeting is a great opportunity to be in community and reflect on your experiences so far, share any challenges, and celebrate the progress you've made. It’s a time to reconnect, share feedback, and make sure everyone feels supported as we move forward in the program.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Intro to Pre-Health Workshop
description: Join Pre-Professional Advising for our Intro to Pre-Health workshop. This workshop is for all students interested in careers in medicine (MD/DO), dentistry, physician assistant, veterinary medicine, physical therapy, occupational therapy, pharmacy, and optometry. We will discuss the application process, required coursework, and resources available to help you succeed. This workshop is required for first-year students before making an appointment with a pre-professional advisor.

name: UNLV Global Success Series: Adjusting to a New Culture, AKA Culture Shock and What to Expect
description: Valerie Pierce, director of the English Language Center will present "Adjusting to a New Culture, AKA Culture Shock and What to Expect." Did you know there are four stages to culture shock? Let's talk about what stage you, and everyone else around you, are in.
