name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
11th Annual Summit on Nevada Education
location: Stan Fulton Building - International Gaming Institute
description: Join us at this year’s Summit on Nevada Education as we recognize the resilience and determination needed to improve Nevada's education and mental health spaces for students of all ages and stages. We'll explore a wide array of topics, including education funding, private and charter school approaches, and celebrate innovative partnerships and effective approaches that are driving positive change in our schools and communities.

name: Arizona Hot Springs Trek
description: Join Outdoor Adventures as we explore the natural wonder of Arizona Hot Springs. This 6-mile round-trip, moderately challenging hike winds through a stunning slot canyon and leads to a naturally occurring hot spring along the beautiful Colorado River in Arizona. The trip includes instruction, transportation, food, and all necessary equipment.

name: Conversational Chinese: Speak Like a Local!
description: Interested in learning Chinese in a fun, welcoming, and stress-free environment? Join the Sands Institute for Chinese Language and Culture at UNLV for a free, beginner-friendly Conversational Chinese course. Designed for those with no prior experience, this course focuses on practical, real-life language skills you can use right away. Participants will learn pronunciation using Pinyin and practice everyday conversations such as introductions, ordering food, shopping, asking for directions, and talking about dates and time through interactive activities and role-playing.

name: UNLV/CSN Transfer Program (UCTP) Workshop
description: Join us at our upcoming UNLV/CSN Transfer Program (UCTP) workshop! The transfer workshop will cover the transfer process, application deadlines, campus resources, upcoming UNLV events, and how credits earned at CSN will transfer to UNLV. We will highlight important transfer information for the different UNLV majors. This workshop is an opportunity to meet a UNLV transition advisor and address any transfer questions. Our in-person workshop will also have a credit review.

name: Henderson Symphony Orchestra: A French Valentine
description: The Henderson Symphony Orchestra, under the direction of Artistic Advisor and Conductor Taras Krysa, present A French Valentine featuring one of the greatest symphonic masterpieces, French composer Hector Berlioz’ Symphony Fantastique.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Burrowing Owl Den Clean Up
location: Corner of Donald Nelson & Rio Vista
description: Volunteer with the UNLV Birding and Conservation Club and UNLVolunteers Rainbow Owl Preserve to help clean out owl burrows!

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
USAC Summer 2027 Visiting Professor Application Webinar
description: This webinar is meant to go over the USAC Visiting Professor Program and application details. This webinar will be recorded and shared upon request.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Decolonizing Data Collection and Management
description: This session introduces key principles of decolonizing data and examines how colonial practices continue to shape common data collection and management workflows. Participants will explore how applying a decolonial lens to data work can surface power dynamics, center community context, and support more ethical and reparative approaches to data collection, stewardship, and use.

name: Anthro Proseminar Series: "Sherds to Gables: Path to Preservation"
description: Art Krupicz, Nevada State Historic Preservation Officer, will give a lecture titled ""From Sherds to Gables: One Archaeologist's Path to Preservation" for the Anthropology Department's Spring 2026 Proseminar Series.

name: Queer Sex Ed
description: Join UNLV's Gender Care Team for a queer sex positive journey of how to stay safe while enjoying sexuality and kinks.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: Intro to Pre-Law Workshop
description: Interested in a law career but not sure where to start? Come to the Intro to Pre-Law workshop to find out more about careers in, and applying to, law programs. We will leave time for a 15 minute Q & A session after the completion of the workshop. This workshop is required for first-year students before making an appointment with a pre-professional advisor.

name: Finger Knitting Butterflies
description: Come by the Lied Library Makerspace to learn how to finger knit your very own butterfly. All supplies and training will be supplied. This workshop does not require an orientation however, we do highly recommend attending one for extended use of the space after these event.

name: Success Series: Building Your Blueprint #2
description: We’ll cover the basics of setting effective goals, share helpful tools and resources available at UNLV, and leave time for questions and reflection. The UNLV Writing Center will also join us to discuss writing habits and organizational strategies, helping students plan writing tasks, manage assignments, and stay organized throughout the semester. By the end of the session, students will walk away with a clearer vision and a personalized blueprint to guide their semester.

name: Lied Library Video Studio Orientation
description: Discover the essentials of video production in this hands-on orientation with our library multimedia team. Step into our state-of-the-art Video Production Studio and learn how to bring your creative projects to life. In just 30 minutes, you'll get practical experience with:

name: NEXTET presents: Timothy Hoft performs Rzewski's 'The People United Will Never Be Defeated!'
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Doc Rando Hall
description: NEXTET, the contemporary chamber music series at the UNLV School of Music features 20th and 21st-century concert music performed by faculty, students, and guest artists.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Vibes and Verse: An Introduction to Natural Language Processing (NLP) Using Poetry
description: "Vibes and Verse" introduces students to natural language processing (NLP) techniques for exploring patterns, emotion, and meaning in written texts, with a focus on poetry and love letters. Participants will learn how to analyze word frequencies, conduct sentiment analysis, and create simple visualizations using accessible NLP tools, gaining hands-on experience in interpreting and visualizing textual data.

name: Grad Rebel Advantage: Considering Grad School? Let’s Talk Funding
description: Grad school can be an exciting next step, but figuring out how to pay for it can feel overwhelming. This workshop is designed for undergrads who are considering graduate school and want to explore their funding options. We’ll break down the different ways you can finance your education—like assistantships, scholarships, fellowships, financial aid, and outside funding sources.

name: TILT and the Transparency of Pedagogy: A Faculty Panel Presentation
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 240.
description: Transparency in Learning and Teaching (TILT) provides a teaching framework that clarifies course assignments' purpose, tasks, and criteria (e.g., the "how" and the "why") in support of deeper learning, mastery of skills, academic confidence, and belongingness. You are invited to join us for a faculty panel featuring David Copeland, Sharon Jalene, and Elaine Bunker, as they share their own TILT experiences in the UNLV classroom. The panel will be moderated by Dan Gianoutsos, Assistant Vice Provost for Academic Learning Services.

name: BEST Career Expo Prep Session #2
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        BEH 239
description: Lee Business School presents the BEST Career Expo Workshop Series: Master the Art of Networking and Job Securing. Gear up for the BEST Career Expo with our comprehensive workshop series designed to equip students with the essential tools to connect with employers and secure your dream job. Each workshop provides you an opportunity to gain invaluable insights from industry experts and employers who will guide you through key aspects of career preparation. All UNLV students are invited to participate.

name: Cards, Consoles & Campaigns: A Library Gaming Meet-Up
description: Join the University Libraries for a drop-in gaming meet-up. Whether you’re new to TTRPGs, into deck-building, prefer puzzles, or enjoy Mario Kart, there’s space for every type of player. Bring a game you love, try something new, and connect with others who share your interests. All experience levels are welcome. Snacks, board games, TTRPG campaigns, and Nintendo Switches provided!

name: Graduate College: Where Does the Time Go? Managing Your Time While Harnessing your Power
location: Hybrid: Virtual & at the UNLV Graduate College Gather rooms 268 & 269
description: This workshop will go beyond the basics of time management and help you maximize the limited time you have each day. You’ll learn how to manage your intellect, energy, and stress while managing your workload and checking things off your to-do list. Lunch will be provided.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Intuitive Eating Group at CICFC
location:
Paul McDermott Physical Education - Building A
        Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling
MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Between the Lines: Black History Month Crochet-a-Thon
description: Celebrate Black History Month with Student Connection and Engagement, UNLV CAPS, and the UNLV Wellness Zone at our cozy Crochet-a-Thon! This event offers a relaxing and creative space to learn or practice crochet, fostering mindfulness and reducing stress – key aspects of emotional wellness.

name: Welcome Spring 2026 General Meeting | International Student Organization
description: Join us as we kick off Spring 2026 with our first general meeting of the semester! This gathering is a great opportunity for all members, new and returning, to connect, meet the leadership team, and learn about upcoming events, programs, and ways to get involved. We will be sharing important updates, welcoming new students, and creating a space to celebrate the diverse cultures that make our organization special. Come build friendships, share ideas, and start the semester strong with our global community at UNLV.

name: Bachelor of Social Work Degree Online Information Session
description: The School of Social Work will host an online information session for prospective students interested in applying to the Bachelor of Social Work degree. Applications for the Bachelor of Social Work degree will open March 1, 2026 for the fall 2026 semester. Representatives from the Bachelor of Social Work program and advising department department will help explain the program, requirements and application questions.

name: The Peer Mentor Experience: Insights and Opportunities
description: Hear from peer mentors about their experience and learn how to apply to be a peer mentor!

name: First General Meeting: UNLV Maternal and Child Health Student Organization
location:
University Gateway Building
        Hybrid: GTW 318 & Zoom
description: The UNLV Maternal and Child Health Student Organization (MACHSO) is a new interdisciplinary Graduate & Professional Student Organization made up of UNLV students, alumni, faculty, and community members. Our mission is to promote and create resources and events that improve Maternal and Child Health in Southern Nevada by strengthening leadership, networks, mentoring, and education for all who are interested in this field.We invite students from all majors and levels to attend our first general meeting and seminar on Tuesday, February 10, from 3–4 p.m. in GTW 318.

name: The Rise of the Global Middle Class
description: As part of the Brookings Scholar Lecture Series, Brookings Mountain West presents a lecture titled, "The Rise of the Global Middle Class" by Brookings senior fellow in global economy and development, Homi Kharas.

name: Virtual: Master of Science in Hotel Administration Information Session
description: Interested in Hotel Administration? Join us to learn more about what the Master of Hotel Administration at UNLV has to offer! The 31-hour Master of Science in Hotel Administration degree program will prepare you for a successful career as an upper-level executive in the hospitality industry. The typical completion time for full-time students is 1 year; part-time students usually take 2 years to complete the program.

name: Podcasting 101: Basics of Recording and Editing
description: Join David Ramos Candelas, multimedia and design specialist, to learn the basics of podcast recording and editing using technology found inside of Lied Library's Multimedia Production Studios. During this one-hour workshop, you will explore Lied Library's multimedia resources and practice basic recording and editing. Seating is limited.

name: Leadership Link Up : Jimmy Neutron – “Think, Don’t Panic!”
description: Got a leadership emergency? Channel your inner Jimmy Neutron and learn how to think before you panic. In this high-energy session, we’ll explore how stress affects decision-making and how to stay creative and calm in chaos. Participants will: Examine how stress affects decision-making and contributes to reactive leadership.​ Learn strategies to pause, assess, and choose thoughtful responses during high-pressure moments.​ Practice applying a problem-solving model to complex, emotionally charged leadership challenges.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Swiping Right on Your Data: Exploring Humanities and Cultural Datasets
description: "Swiping Right on Your Data" is a fun, Love Data Week workshop that introduces students to intriguing datasets from the humanities, arts, and cultural studies. Participants will explore historical archives, literary corpora, and digital museum records, discovering the fascinating stories hidden in publicly available data. Along the way, they will learn how to evaluate, compare, and “match” with the right dataset, while generating ideas for research projects, digital humanities work, or creative data-driven storytelling.

name: Beginners' Idea Bridge Workshop Series: Copyright for Creators
description: This workshop will teach you how to distinguish copyright in the marketplace, including common exceptions to copyright, as well as how copyright protection works for creators, including how to navigate obtaining permission to use works of copyright holders. You will partake in various learning activities to increase your understanding of copyright topics.

name: Webinar: W-4 Updates and Changes to Tax Withholding
description: Each year, many individuals must deal with the complexities and challenges of their US tax situation and tax filing. The IRS significantly redesigned Form W-4 in 2020 to align with the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017, which has caused many people to become frustrated with their tax withholding. At the end of this webinar, you should be able to understand the purpose of and how the new W-4 form works, familiarize yourself with recent tax law changes, determine whether your tax withholding is correct or how to adjust it, and more.

name: Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Workshop
description: Feeling a little confused about Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) and what it means for your financial aid? No worries, you're not alone! Come to "From Criteria to Clarity: Navigating SAP Standards and the Appeal Process" webinar. We'll break down the SAP policy for keeping your financial aid in simple terms. Plus, we'll show you exactly what to do if you ever need to appeal. Get the info you need to stay on track and keep your aid flowing!

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Surviving and Thriving Student Panel
description: An open, student-led panel that helps guide first-year students on their college journeys. Upper-level students majoring in the College of Liberal Arts (COLA) will answer a series of questions about their college careers that will teach first-year students on how to be successful at UNLV. There will be a segment at the end where the audience can ask any questions that they feel best benefits their individual lives. Participants will be provided free snacks and COLA swag.

name: Recovery Curious
location:
Student Services Complex - Building A
        CAPS Suite 143
description: Are you curious about substance use recovery or ways to reduce use? Join us virtually or in-person to share questions or receive support around the topic of substance use and recovery.

name: Success Series: Effective Communication
description: In this Success Series session, Lean-In to Effective Communication, students will explore the importance of clear, intentional communication and how it supports success throughout the semester. We’ll also discuss how to clearly express your needs, navigate different communication styles, and highlight helpful ASC resources to support you along the way. The UNLV Writing Center will join to provide general tips on clarity in writing and professional email communication, helping you communicate confidently across campus.

name: New Employee & Benefits Orientation
description: The university provides a competitive benefits package to benefit eligible employees. During new employee and benefits orientation, we provide an introduction to UNLV and important information about its history and culture; policies and procedures; services and resources available to employees; workplace protection; health, retirement, and voluntary benefits; and more.

name: Virtual: International Student Guide to Graduate School
description: To ensure a smooth transition, the Graduate Recruitment team is here to answer any questions you may inquire regarding your next steps to completing your application at UNLV.

name: It's Just Coffee: Evolving World of Work
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups (approximately once a month) for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning.

name: Blind Date with a Book
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        Room 101
description: Wrap and decorate a book you love but are okay with parting with. You'll "speed date" with other students, pitching your mystery book to each other in fun, quick rounds. No covers, no spoliers. Just vibes, food, and your next great read!

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Valentine’s Day Crafting & Jeopardy con LGSA
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building B
        Room 225
description: Join the Latinx Graduate Student Association for crafting while honoring influential Afro-Latinx figures with round of Jeopardy. Come to get creative, show off your trivia skills, or just enjoy the company! We would love to see you there.

name: Klai Juba Wald Spring 2026 Lecture Series - Populous
location:
Paul B. Sogg Architecture Building
        ARC 127 (Auditorium)
description: Geoff Cheong (Senior Principal and Architect) and Karl Marsh (Architect) from Populous will deliver a lecture at 6 p.m. on February 11 in the School of Architecture building on the UNLV campus. The event, which is free and open to the public, is part of the Klai Juba Wald Lecture Series. A 5:30 p.m. reception will precede the lecture.

name: Learn to Stand-Up Paddleboard
description: Learn to stand-up paddleboard in the controlled environment of the SRWC pool! Our experienced trip leaders will guide you through a two-hour course on how to properly use a stand-up paddleboard.

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: Good Trouble, Necessary Trouble
location: Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
description: This panel discussion explores Black resistance, civil disobedience, and collective action across past, present, and future movements. Participants are invited to engage in dialogue around activism, leadership, and social change. Food will be provided. Open to everyone. In partnership with the LEAD Team. More details and RSVP

name: Clark County School District: Middle School Honor Bands
description: Students from around the Las Vegas Valley audition to participate in the honor bands. They rehearse together (with guest conductors) over 2 days, then they perform in this concert.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Countdown to Commencement for Graduate Students
description: Join us for Countdown to Commencement, a dynamic hybrid event for all UNLV graduate and professional students who are preparing for their final semester! How can graduate students apply to graduate? What does the process look like? What resources are available? What forms and items are necessary to complete graduation and participate in commencement? This event is geared at answering these questions and more in order to help graduate students prepare to graduate successfully and on time.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: RSI Workshop Series: Direct Instruction – Improving Interaction & Engagement Within Your Courses
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Direct Instruction. This training will focus on the following outcomes:

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Tax Returns: Easier Than Your Finals!
description: Forget all-nighters and exam stress—this tax workshop is truly Easier Than Your Finals! We'll break down the federal tax return process into simple, repeatable steps using the essential Form 1040. You will master your W-2 and your 1098-T tuition forms, figure out your dependency status, and calculate your adjusted gross income, standard deduction, and taxable income. Use practical examples to walk through the exact steps for calculating your biggest possible refund, so you can get your cash back faster and file with absolute confidence.

name: Melanin, Memory, Mirror: Healing from Colorism and Texturism in the Black Community
description: Real Talk is a monthly facilitated dialogue series that creates space for honest conversation, shared learning, and reflection. In recognition of Black History Month, this session will be facilitated by Dr. Valerie Taylor (UNLV Interdisciplinary Studies) and will focus on colorism and texturism and their impact on Black identity, beauty standards, and self-worth. Lunch will be provided. Open to everyone. More details and RSVP.

name: Wellness & Service: Safer Sex Kit Building
description: Join UNLVolunteers and the Wellness Zone for our annual Safer Sex Kit Building event, a long-standing tradition of service and education. Participants will assemble safer sex kits for the community while engaging in an informative conversation about safer sex practices. This hands-on event combines community care with essential health education, empowering individuals to prioritize their well-being and the well-being of others.

name: Neuroscience Interdisciplinary Research Area (iRDA) Meet Up
description: Neuroscience is a diverse field that investigates the organization, development, and function of the nervous system. It is a fast growing field with research ranging from mechanisms responsible for basic neuronal function and development to investigations of behavior, cognition, and neurological/psychiatric disorders.

name: Lecture: “Privacy and Private Life in Jewish and Christian Ego Documents in the Early Modern Netherlands”
location:
John S. Wright Hall - Building B
        History Department Conference Room (WRI-B 326)
description: The Department of History research seminar and the Jewish Studies steering committee invite all interested members of the UNLV community to a lecture on "Privacy and Private Life in Jewish and Christian Ego Documents in the Early Modern Netherlands."

name: Becoming an Adaptive Perfectionist
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 1502 (Health Education Room)
description: Learn how to rethink the relationship between motivation and self-criticism, set manageable goals, and be more productive while also tending to your wellbeing.

name: Global Thursday: Culture and Conversation
description: Learn about different cultures and share your own, enjoy international snacks, and make new friends every Global Thursday. All UNLV students are welcome!

name: Technology Entrepreneurship Evening — Unlock the Potential (TEE-UP)
description: Join us for a new monthly evening event series connecting UNLV students with successful technology entrepreneurs! The April TEE-UP event will feature keynote speaker Piotr Tomasik, '09 BS Computer Science, co-founder and president of Tensorwave. What to Expect:

name: GPSA Council Meeting 45-09
description: This is our first council meeting for the year. We cannot wait to see you all! The meeting will be hybrid: - In-person at the SU 208B/C - Online via Zoom (Only council members will receive the Zoom link). However, members of the public can watch the meeting via a YouTube live stream.

name: University Forum Lecture: Letters to the Editor: Writing in Support of Democracy in This Time
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: Nationally recognized anthropologist, Dr. Alejandro Lugo, will explore how "Letters to the Editor" can shape public discourse, support a free press, and encourage dialogue on a broad range of pressing topics within and beyond the 'Ivory Tower.' Notably, Dr. Lugo is a recipient of the American Anthropological Association's Anthropology in Media Award (AIME) winner, and New Mexico State University's Star of Arts and Sciences Award. The event is co-sponsored by the College of Education's Dr. Porter Lee Troutman Jr. Center for Multicultural Education.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Nursing: Final Day to Completely Withdraw
Responsible Conduct of Research Training
description: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) training is an essential component to make certain that the research activities of UNLV faculty, staff, and students are conducted with integrity and in compliance with regulatory guidance. RCR is defined by The National Institutes of Health (NIH) as the practice of scientific investigation with integrity. It involves the awareness and application of established professional norms and ethical principles in the performance of all activities related to scientific research.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: Douglass Day Transcribe-a-thon
description: Join the University Libraries and the Department of Interdisciplinary, Gender and Ethnic Studies for UNLV's Douglass Day celebration, an annual program that marks the birth of Frederick Douglass. Each year for Douglass Day, thousands of people nationwide gather to help create new and freely available resources for learning about Black history by transcribing documents from the Library of Congress.

name: Dear Data Party
description: Bring your data, your curiosity, and your creativity to a chill gathering focused on drawing, chatting, and making sense of data by hand. Create "Dear Data"-inspired doodles, connect with others, and enjoy some sweets along the way.

name: Relaxing Your Mind: Coping With Anxiety
description: This two-part workshop is intended to provide information about anxiety and a variety of practical coping skills for anxiety and stress.

name: UNLV Women's Council Fellowship Lunch
description: Join fellow Women’s Council members for lunch the second Friday of the month at the on-campus Dining Commons (DC).

name: Teaching @ UNLV: Applying AI Strategies in Your Online Teaching Series - Course Design and Facilitation
description: As part of the Applying AI Strategies in Your Online Teaching series, this session will focus on course design and facilitation techniques using AI. During this 45-minute virtual conversation, faculty will explore and discuss practical strategies, tools, and resources for integrating AI into their teaching practices.

name: APC 101: How Do I Publish an Open Access Article
description: Curious about open access publishing and article processing charges (APCs)? This workshop guides participants through the essentials of open access, including what APCs are, how they impact scholarly publishing, and strategies for covering publishing costs. Participants will also learn how to navigate library resources and align their publishing practices with open access best practices, taking their research from idea to manuscript with confidence. This workshop is part of the Rebel+ Libraries Workshop Series.

name: Handmade Hearts: A Cricut Craft Social
description: Join us for a cozy, creativity-filled Valentine’s craft event! Whether you’re making a card for your bestie, your crush, or just celebrating self-love, we’ve got everything you need. Use our Cricut machines to design and cut your own custom Valentine’s Day cards. All materials will be provided, just bring your creativity. This will be a chill, social space to craft, chat, and unwind. No experience is needed and beginners are welcome.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: UNLV Global Success Series: Live Like a Local: Vegas Edition
description: Join Sam Lee and Kaila Seidler from the Hospitality College as they prepare you to get ready to discover Las Vegas like a true local! In this session, we'll discuss the city's hidden gems, budget-friendly fun, and insider tips that go beyond the typical tourist traps. Learn how to truly experience Vegas, from amazing food to unique entertainment, without breaking the bank.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Building Tour
description: In 2022, the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV opened its new educational facility in the Las Vegas Medical District. The Office of Admissions is now offering a building tour program for those considering medical education (MD) degree studies.

name: Faculty Senate General Education Spring Meeting 2
description: Regular monthly meeting of the Faculty Senate General Education Committee

name: “Socially Centered Discourse”
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room C122
description: Ravi Thakral, Dept. of Philosophy, University of Nevada, Reno—People often preface a claim by foregrounding part of their identity: “Speaking as an American…”, “Speaking as an immigrant…”, “Speaking as an educator…”. Although these locutions are widespread in everyday and public discourse, they are surprisingly under-theorized as a distinctive linguistic device. These phrases do more than add background.

name: Chai Social and General Body Meeting
description: Join the Indian Students Association for a cozy and engaging Chai Social combined with our General Body Meeting. This event is a great opportunity to connect with fellow students, learn more about ISA’s upcoming plans, and share your ideas about events you want us to organize in a relaxed setting. The event will begin with a fun icebreaker to help everyone get to know one another. Enjoy chai and samosa while playing board games and bingo with old and new friends.

name: SIHS Alumni Chapter Night with the Las Vegas Desert Dogs
location:
Lee's Family Forum
200 S. Green Valley Pkwy
Henderson, NV 89012
description: Join us for an exciting night of fast-paced lacrosse as the Las Vegas Desert Dogs hit the field — and enjoy an exclusive offer! The SIHS offer includes: Special discounted game ticket A Rebels Desert Dogs Experience An opportunity to give back to the School of Integrated Health Sciences

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
7th Annual Las Vegas Regional Essentially Ellington Final Concert
description: Jazz Outreach Initiative and UNLV School of Music, Division of Jazz & Commercial Music present the 7th Annual Las Vegas Regional Essentially Ellington High School Jazz Band Festival Final Concert! The final Saturday evening concert will feature the award-winning UNLV Jazz Ensemble 1 under the direction of Dave Loeb and Nathan Tanouye, including featured select student soloists from some of the nearly 20 participating high school big bands from across the western United States.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Family Swim at SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during this time. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Webinar: Importance of Mental Health Awareness in the Black/African American Community
description: In honor of Black History Month, this webinar will explore the mental health needs of the Black community and the importance of culturally competent care. This webinar will explore important topics related to mental health for the Black/African American community, such as myths about Black mental health, racial trauma, and working while Black. It will also discuss ways to embrace Black mental health, understanding culturally competent care, and resources to support mental health and well-being in the Black/African American community.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Curricular Practical Training Workshop
description: Join us and learn how to engage your Curricular Practical Training (CPT) benefit. CPT is the legal pathway for F-1 students who wish to participate in paid work experience or internships. Attend this short informational session to find out all your options and how to take advantage of this wonderful F-1 benefit.

name: RSI Workshop Series: Information & Questions – Providing Information and Responding to Questions
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Providing Information and Responding to Questions. This training will focus on the following outcomes:

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 235 (Faculty Lounge)
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Intuitive Eating Group at CICFC
location:
Paul McDermott Physical Education - Building A
        Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling
MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Coffee with Karly
description: Join us for Coffee with Karly, a casual and welcoming space to build authentic connections, reflect on your leadership journey, and explore how relationships and mentorship shape who we are as leaders. These monthly conversations offer the opportunity to engage in meaningful dialogue, ask questions, and share experiences that strengthen your leadership skills and sense of community. Through Coffee with Karly, you will:

name: How to Receive Criticism and Make It Work for You
description: Receiving criticism is an important and valuable skill. To achieve success in any endeavor we must be able to tolerate critical feedback and learn from it. However, receiving criticism is difficult for most people. Upon receiving criticism we feel vulnerable and become defensive and thereby miss opportunities to benefit from feedback. This workshop will help you explore why we become defensive, deliver tips for knowing when your “hot button” has been pushed and how to accept and integrate useful criticism. The training will also instruct though examples and/or role plays.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: From Chaos to Calm: Aromatherapy
description: Join us in creating your own aromatherapy inhaler to take home after attending this workshop. Learn about the benefits of essential oil aromatherapy for mindfulness and wellness.

name: Administrative Faculty February General Meeting
description: Regular monthly meeting to discuss issues of importance to administrative faculty. All administrative faculty are invited to attend in person or online via Zoom. They can also submit items for the agenda anonymously via our New Business for AFC form.

name: Optional Practical Training (OPT) Workshop
description: Are you graduating this semester? Do you plan or wish to gain work experience post-graduation? Optional Practical Training (OPT) is the legal pathway for any F-1 student who graduates this semester to acquire more work experience in their field of study. If you wish to engage in OPT, you should begin your application process several months prior to graduation. Attend an OPT session as soon as possible to learn about deadlines and restrictions. A Q&A session is part of this training, so bring your questions and learn how to take advantage of this important benefit.

name: Podcasting 101: Basics of Recording & Editing
description: Join David Ramos Candelas, multimedia and design specialist, to learn the basics of podcast recording and editing using technology found inside of Lied Library's Multimedia Production Studios. During this one-hour workshop, you will explore Lied Library's multimedia resources and practice basic recording and editing. Seating is limited.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: Galloping Forward Chinese New Year Celebration
location:
Hospitality Hall
        1st floor atrium and 2nd floor
description: The Sands Institute for Chinese Language and Culture invites faculty and staff to the inaugural 'Galloping Forward' Chinese New Year Celebration, honoring the Year of the Fire Horse, a symbol of energy, confidence, and forward momentum. The event will feature Asian cuisine, festive toasts, a lion dance, Sichuan Opera face-changing, Chinese music, a fashion show, and prize drawings, including a M-Resort staycation package and restaurant gift cards, as the Sands Institute looks to build a vibrant new campus tradition.

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Admissions Information Session
description: These virtual information sessions are for individuals who are starting to consider medical school as a graduate degree option and career path. We welcome high school seniors, community college and university students, or other individuals just beginning to research medical school and/or beginning to plan for their application. We will offer an overview of the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV, resources to help consider medical school, details regarding application, and a Q&A at the end of the session.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Webinar: Hiring an Attorney
description: Dealing with a legal issue can stressful, but finding and hiring the right attorney shouldn’t be. In this webinar, we will discuss some of the basic areas of law and billing terminology that can help you feel more prepared for your initial consultation with an attorney.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Tenure and Promotion Information Forum
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        Room 101
description: The Office of Faculty Affairs is hosting a Tenure and Promotion Information Forum that will provide an overview of the tenure and promotion process, including policies and timeline. Potential candidates and those involved in the review process are encouraged to attend. Representatives from the Office of the Executive Vice President & Provost will be on hand to answer questions.

name: Working Effectively on Online Group Projects
description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. Online group projects can be challenging without the right tools and strategies. This session helps students learn how to collaborate effectively using UNLV’s built-in tools.

name: Beginners' Idea Bridge Workshop Series: Viability of a Trademark
description: This workshop will teach you about confusingly similar trademarks, the strength of trademarks, and how to search for trademarks in the USPTOs database. You will partake in learning activities to increase your understanding of trademark topics.

name: Dissertation Defense: Julia Maranville
description: Julia Maranville, Ph.D. Candidate Department of Counselor Education, School Psychology, and Human Services Parental Perceptions of Communication Barriers With Healthcare Providers When Expressing Early Concerns About Autism in Rural and Frontier Areas Advisory Committee Members Scott Loe, Advisory Committee Chair Katherine Lee, Advisory Committee Member Katherine Feather, Advisory Committee Member Joseph Morgan, Advisory Committee Member

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Pre Med Mentoring Sessions for 1st Gen and URM Students
description: Mentoring sessions will be available for first generation and underrepresented minority pre-medicine students from UNLV, UNR, NSC, and CSN who are interested in learning about general admissions for the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV. These students will have the opportunity to meet with medical students from the school of medicine, as well as representatives from the school of medicine's Office of Community Affairs.

name: Cayuse SP Training for Proposals
description: For new PI's and any PI who has not previously attended a Cayuse SP training session. Cayuse SP will integrate with the research enterprise Cayuse IRB platform as well as Workday HRM and allow for a more streamlined and efficient connection between systems. Cayuse SP offers a broad array of benefits, including:

name: Lied Library Video Studio Orientation
description: Discover the essentials of video production in this hands-on orientation with our library multimedia team. Step into our state-of-the-art Video Production Studio and learn how to bring your creative projects to life. In just 30 minutes, you'll get practical experience with:

name: Thriving Authentically: Black Voices in the Workplace
description: Thriving Authentically is a monthly career-focused series designed to support professional development, connection, and growth. In recognition of Black History Month, this session will center navigating professional spaces while staying true to identity and values through a panel and networking experience. Lunch will be provided. Open to everyone. In partnership with Career Services and Workforce Development. More details and RSVP

name: BSN Program Virtual Information Session
description: UNLV Nursing welcomes students interested in pursuing a career in nursing to join a virtual informational session on February 18 from 1 p.m. to 2 p.m. to learn more about the pre-licensure program. This session will outline the pathway from pre-requisite work to admission to the School of Nursing program, provide an overview of the program, courses, clinical rotations, and resources, and include support from student services, the Student Nurse Association, and more. Prospective students will also have the opportunity to ask questions directly to the program directors.

name: First Gen Forward: Dorm Sweet Dorm
description: Join us for a student to student workshop that will share insight on how to make the most of life on campus from one first-gen student to another!

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Lunar New Year Night Market
description: The Sands Institute for Chinese Language and Culture at UNLV is hosting a Lunar New Year Night Market featuring engaging cultural presentations, a lion dance, exciting performances, activity booths, free food, and more. Students, faculty, and staff are invited to celebrate and welcome the Year of the Fire Horse, a symbol of energy, confidence, and forward momentum.

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: Working Effectively on Online Group Projects
description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. Online group projects can be challenging without the right tools and strategies. This session helps students learn how to collaborate effectively using UNLV’s built-in tools.

name: UNLV Symphony Orchestra: Music of Richard Strauss
description: The UNLV Symphony Orchestra, conducted by the orchestra's artistic director Taras Krysa, performs music from Richard Strauss.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Donuts with Office of Economic Development
description: Drop by for donuts and discover how OED can support your innovation. Join the Office of Economic Development (OED) for an informal morning session to learn how we can help bring your ideas to market. What We'll Cover:

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Deadlifts and Donuts
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        First Floor Lobby
description: Come challenge yourself with our deadlifts and donuts challenge! Participants are welcome to complete as many reps as possible in one bout. After finishing, enjoy a donut as a fun treat to celebrate your effort and help refuel your body.

name: Anthropology Open House
location:
John S. Wright Hall - Building C
        Anthropology Labs
description: The department of Anthropology will celebrate Anthropology Day 2026 by hosting tours and activities in the Anthropology research labs.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: QGIS Workshop Series: GIS Fundamentals
description: This foundational workshop introduces participants to Geographic Information Systems (GIS), covering core concepts and the role of GIS across a range of disciplines. Participants will learn the basics of spatial data and gain hands-on experience navigating the QGIS interface, building a practical foundation for working with geospatial information. The four-week QGIS Workshop Series provides an overview of QGIS, a free and open-source GIS system, and how you can integrate it with your research.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Success Series: Self Care #1
description: In this workshop, you will learn how to prioritize self-care to maintain a healthy balance between your academic and personal responsibilities. This workshop will provide you with strategies and techniques to manage stress, boost your energy, and improve your overall well-being. This workshop will also provide you with tools and strategies to tap into your motivation, develop a growth mindset, and take action towards self-improvement.

name: Virtual: Grad 101 - Ready for Grad School & How to Apply
description: Considering graduate school? This information session will examine the various personal and professional benefits associated with pursuing graduate education at UNLV. Join us to discuss getting ready for grad School. In this information session we will:

name: 3D Printed Fidget Toy Workshop
description: Learn how to make your own 3D printed fidget toys at this workshop! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D fidget toys printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. User must have completed the makerspace safety orientation and signed the user agreement before using the space. The 3D printers are available on a first-come, first-served basis, and are available any time the makerspace is open.

name: Curiosity Compass: Mapping Your Path to Meaningful Research
description: This interactive workshop invites students to explore their natural curiosity and understand how it connects to academic disciplines. Participants will take a curiosity assessment to identify their personal curiosity style, reflect on information they naturally gravitate toward in everyday life, and discover how these interests align with scholarly fields.

name: Virtual: Grad 201 Admissions - Criteria, Context, and Considerations
description: Need help with your grad application? Join us for a workshop to assist with your graduate application. Join us for a workshop on common elements of graduate school applications. In this workshop, we will:

name: Podcasting 102: Audio Mixing
description: Join David Ramos Candelas, Multimedia and Design Specialist, to learn how to polish your podcast using audio editing tools like de-noisers and EQ. During this one-hour workshop, you will explore Lied Library's multimedia resources and practice audio mixing.

name: BEST Career Expo Prep Session #3
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 239
description: Lee Business School presents the BEST Career Expo Workshop Series: Master the Art of Networking and Job Securing Gear up for the BEST Career Expo with our comprehensive workshop series designed to equip students with the essential tools to connect with employers and secure your dream job. Each workshop provides you an opportunity to gain invaluable insights from industry experts and employers who will guide you through key aspects of career preparation. All UNLV students are invited to participate.

name: Becoming an Adaptive Perfectionist
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 1502 (Health Education Room)
description: Learn how to rethink the relationship between motivation and self-criticism, set manageable goals, and be more productive while also tending to your wellbeing.

name: Global Thursday: Language Exchange
description: Interested in learning or practicing a new language? Do you love exploring language-culture connections? Join us every second Global Thursday for the Language Exchange Program (LEP) where students can explore languages such as Chinese, German, Vietnamese, Persian, Japanese, and more in a relaxed environment. No prior study required!

name: 2026 Juanita Greer White Distinguished Lecture Series: "RaceCAR-M, an Emerging Improvement in Cancer Immunotherapy"
description: The UNLV School of Life Sciences invites you to the 2026 White Lecture, which is free and open to the public. "RaceCAR-M, an Emerging Improvement in Cancer Immunotherapy"

name: Rebels After Dark
description: Join REB for our late night event of the semester! Free food and activities for all UNLV students.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Thesis Defense: Emily Christensen
location:
Advanced Engineering Building
        Room 351 and Virtual
description: Emily Christensen, MSEG Candidate Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Construction Estimating Pre- and Post-fire Soil Erosion for Wildfires using Remote Sensing and the RUSLE Model Advisory Committee Members Haroon Sahotra, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Sajjad Ahmad, Ph.D., P.E., Advisory Committee Co-Chair Erica Marti, Ph.D., M.Ed., Advisory Committee Member Venkatesan Muthukumar, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: Webinar: Eldercare In-Home Support and Services
description: We recognize that for many caregivers, the ability to keep a loved one with care needs safely in the home and prevent or prolong the need for long term care placement is an important goal. To that end, this webinar will help you understand the range of services and supports available to help older adults and individuals with disabilities remain safely and comfortably in their homes. Key topics discussed will include in-home care options, community-based resources and caregiver support programs, and various payment and funding options.

name: Responsible Conduct of Research Training
description: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) training is an essential component to make certain that the research activities of UNLV faculty, staff, and students are conducted with integrity and in compliance with regulatory guidance. RCR is defined by The National Institutes of Health (NIH) as the practice of scientific investigation with integrity. It involves the awareness and application of established professional norms and ethical principles in the performance of all activities related to scientific research.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: LeaderShape: Resilience Workshop
description: Just like Alex, Marty, Gloria, and Melman had to adapt when they found themselves far from the comforts of the Central Park Zoo, resilience is all about learning how to navigate the wild twists and turns of life.

name: Art Exhibition: Moth Ball
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Window Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Moth Ball, an installation of a single risograph print in the Museum’s Window Gallery.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Art Exhibition: Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Center Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt, an exhibition of memorial cyanotypes by Amy Elkins.

name: Art Exhibition: El camino se traza al andar (The Path is Traced by Walking)
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art West Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking), an exhibition of artworks by the Los Angeles-based interdisciplinary artist Jackie Amézquita.

name: Department of Economics Seminar Series: Jeremy Piger, University of Oregon
description: The Department of Economics would like to invite you to a seminar by professor Jeremy Piger from the University of Oregon. Piger’s research interests are in applied macroeconomics, including studying business cycles and inflation. He previously worked as an economist at the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis and at the Federal Reserve Board of Governors.

name: Relaxing Your Mind: Coping With Anxiety
description: This two-part workshop is intended to provide information about anxiety and a variety of practical coping skills for anxiety and stress.

name: Study Abroad Scholarships: Part 2 - Freeman Asia & Gilman Drop In Advising
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        C129
description: Learn how to apply for the Gilman and Freeman-ASIA scholarships—both need-based and perfect for study abroad students; we'll cover eligibility, deadlines, and help review your essays and applications. It’s an easy way to boost your chances for free money to go abroad.

name: Success Series: Advising #1
description: Learn why it’s important to schedule advising appointments early and how to make the most of these sessions. We’ll discuss strategies for selecting courses that align with your goals and share tips for effectively utilizing advising resources.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: MSQF Finance Seminar Series: Jarrad Harford—University of Washington
description: The Department of Finance and the Master of Science in Quantitative Finance program would like to invite you to a seminar by Professor Jarrad Harford from University of Washington. He is the Paul Pigott–PACCAR Professor of Finance at the University of Washington’s Foster School of Business, where he recently completed a 12-year term as Department Chair.

name: First Strokes: Watercolor Landscapes for Beginners
description: Come paint with the Makerspace! Experiment with watercolor painting techniques. No painting experience is necessary and you don't need to be an artist to participate. All supplies and training will be provided.

name: UNLV Global Success Series: Paths After Graduation for F-1 & J-1 Graduate Students
description: Laura Chaney, director of International Student & Scholar Compliance will present "Paths After Graduation for F-1 & J-1 Graduate Students." Wondering what your options are after graduation as an F-1 or J-1 international student? This session will review the various paths available to our F-1 and J-1 international students after graduation including applying for practical training work authorization (OPT/Academic Training), beginning a new program at UNLV, transferring to another school, and returning home.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Opening Reception at the Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art
location: Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
description: Three new exhibitions and one installation will go on view at the Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art this February. You are invited to join us for the opening reception.

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: Pipe Organ Recital by Guest Artist Damin Spritzer
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Internationally acclaimed organist Damin Spritzer appears in the Paul S. Hesselink Organ Concert Series for an evening of music praised for its clarity, color, and expressive depth. A performer, scholar, and recording artist, Spritzer serves as area chair and associate professor of organ at the University of Oklahoma and performs regularly throughout the United States and abroad.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
UNLV Alumni Association Day of Service
location:
Spread the Word Nevada
5373 Arville Street, Las Vegas, NV 89118
description: Rebels, join us for our 2026 Day of Service! Give back to the community as we prepare gently-used books to distribute to Las Vegas students through Spread the Word Nevada. We'll work together to remove stickers and fix minor tears, making books presentable for new readers. Make a lasting impact by helping prepare some of the 70,000 gently used books Spread the Word Nevada distributes each month, bringing joy to local children in need.

name: Community Archives Workshop
description: Join the University Libraries' Special Collections & Archives for our annual one-day Community Archives Workshop, designed to help local organizations protect and share their unique histories. Learn the basics of organizing and preserving your records or discover how to capture community voices through oral history interviews. Our expert archivists will offer hands-on guidance tailored to your project, and each participating organization will receive a free Archives Starter Kit packed with essential supplies to jump-start your preservation efforts.
