name: Las Vegas Camp Fair
description: Whether you're interested in local camp options or looking to explore something new (there will be camps from all across the country exhibiting at the camp fair), the Las Vegas Camp Fair will to provide you with valuable insights and learning opportunities to find a camp best suited for your child this summer.

name: Study, Sip & Socialize with Comadres
location:
Bungalow Coffee Co
201 E Charleston Blvd Suite 180, Las Vegas, NV 89104
description: Join us for our monthly writing session created to support your scholarship, your goals, and your community. Whether you’re working on seminar papers, comprehensive exams, articles, your thesis, or dissertation, this is a dedicated space to write with intention alongside fellow comadres. Build momentum, share encouragement, and connect while navigating graduate school together. Come for the focus, stay for the comunidad.

name: Flute Master of Music Recital, Olivia Remak
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Olivia Remak is proud to present her Master of Music recital, featuring works by Valerie Coleman, Anna Bon di Venezia, Eric Ewazen, Michael Colgrass, and Heitor Villa-Lobos. This recital will feature several collaborators, including cellist Elton Chong, Eduardo Arredondo on horn, pianist Nathan Carterette, and Jonathan Lee on harpsichord.

name: Snowshoe Under the Moon: Mt. Charleston
description: Snowshoe under the stars with Outdoor Adventures as the moon illuminates your path during this beginner-friendly nighttime snowshoeing experience. We’ll enjoy hot chocolate and stargazing in the winter wonderland of Mt. Charleston. No experience necessary. The trip includes transportation, equipment, hot drinks, and instruction.

name: Clark County School District: Elementary Music Orff Festival
description: The 33rd Annual Orff Festival is a collaboration of elementary music programs in the Clark County School District. This performance combines sacred, secular, and popular music from around the world. The teachers hope the audience will enjoy a love of the arts alongside the student performers. The Orff Festival is not only an engaging concert but is also a powerful learning experience for more than 250 CCSD students who participate.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
2026 Corporate Challenge: Range Shoot (All Teams)
location:
Clark County Shooting Complex
11357 N Decatur Blvd
Las Vegas, NV 89131
description: Test your focus, precision, and composure in this exciting marksmanship competition. The Range Shoot Challenge invites participants to demonstrate accuracy and control as they aim for the highest score under guided supervision.

name: Family Swim at SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during this time. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: Harpsichord Recital: Music New and Old
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Harpsichordist Faythe Vollrath presents a recital pairing music from the 16th through 18th centuries with music from living composers. Baroque composers featured include Girolamo Fescobaldi, William Byrd, Jan Pieterszoon Sweelinck, and Jean-Baptiste Forqueray. Contemporary composers on the program include Elinor Armer, James Dorsa, Peter Machajdik, and Nissim Schaul. About the artist:

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Webinar: Being Adaptive in an Ever Changing World
description: “Because we’ve always done it like that,” may not be the best answer to, “Why do we do the things we do?” In our ever-changing world, we are seeing shifts in our markets and economies, our cultural norms, our behaviors, and ourselves. Now is the time to thrive by learning how to be adaptive and to be consistently able to change yourself to accommodate and maximize the benefits of change. We encourage anyone who wants to build skills to be more adaptive inside and outside of work to attend.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: RSI Workshop Series: Feedback – Using Multiple Methods to Provide Meaningful Feedback
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Feedback. This training will focus on the following outcomes: Explore learning theories behind multimedia learning and its efficacy Identify WebCampus multimedia tools for optimal student engagement Identify appropriate places in your course to utilize multimedia

name: Cayuse SP Training for Proposals
description: For new PI's and any PI who has not previously attended a Cayuse SP training session. Cayuse SP will integrate with the research enterprise Cayuse IRB platform as well as Workday HRM and allow for a more streamlined and efficient connection between systems. Cayuse SP offers a broad array of benefits, including:

name: Caring for Community: Perspectives from Forensic Anthropology & Bioarchaeology
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 212
description: Join us for another Spring Proseminar lecture series.

name: Virtual Grad: Masters of Social Work Information Session
description: Interested in obtaining a Masters of Social Work (MSW)? Join us for an information session! The MSW graduate program informational sessions are designed to provide prospective applicants with valuable information about the social work graduate program. These sessions will thoroughly review the application requirements and offer applicants an opportunity to ask questions they may have about the program and the application process.

name: Study Abroad Scholarships: Part 2 - Freeman Asia & Gilman Drop In Advising
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        C125
description: Learn how to apply for the Gilman and Freeman-ASIA scholarships—both need-based and perfect for study abroad students; we'll cover eligibility, deadlines, and help review your essays and applications. It’s an easy way to boost your chances for free money to go abroad.

name: 21st Annual Pro Lecture in Legal History
description: William S. Boyd School of Law presents the 21st Annual Philip Pro Lecture in Legal History – The Constitutional Bind: How Americans Came to Idolize a Document that Fails Them Approved for 1 Nevada CLE credit An eye-opening account of how Americans came to revere the Constitution and what this reverence has meant domestically and around the world.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: It's Just Coffee: Mental Health in America
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups (approximately once a month) for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Nevada Health Centers Mammovan
location:
Campus Services Building
        Pink truck and trailer outside of CSB
description: The Nevada Health Centers Mammovan is coming to your community! Please note the following: Women 40 and over require no referral. Women 39 and under, or men, must present a physician (PCP) referral. Must be at least 366 days since last screening. No minors can be left unattended and they may not accompany you into the exam room. Results are mailed directly to you and your primary care physician/specialist.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Art Exhibition: El camino se traza al andar (The Path is Traced by Walking)
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art West Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking), an exhibition of artworks by the Los Angeles-based interdisciplinary artist Jackie Amézquita.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Art Exhibition: Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Center Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt, an exhibition of memorial cyanotypes by Amy Elkins.

name: Pathways to the R01: Strategies for Success Webinar
description: The Division of Research invites you to attend a webinar provided by our partner, McAllister & Quinn, on the NIH R01 funding opportunity. This webinar offers an overview of the NIH funding ecosystem and practical strategies for preparing a competitive R01 application.

name: Art Exhibition: Moth Ball
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Window Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Moth Ball, an installation of a single risograph print in the Museum’s Window Gallery.

name: Black Mini Con
location: Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
description: Black Mini Con is a signature convening that brings together interactive sessions focused on learning, dialogue, and community connection. In recognition of Black History Month, this mini conference will center Black LGBTQIA+ identity, Black mental health, resilience, and belonging. Lunch will be provided. Open to everyone. More details and RSVP

name: Grad Fair
description: Prospective graduate students are invited to engage directly with college representatives and admissions officers, gain insights into financial aid and graduate assistantships, and explore everything you need to know to kickstart your academic journey. Don’t miss this chance to connect, learn, and plan your future with UNLV graduate programs.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Success Series: Advising #2
description: Learn why it’s important to schedule advising appointments early and how to make the most of these sessions. We’ll discuss strategies for selecting courses that align with your goals and share tips for effectively utilizing advising resources.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Intuitive Eating Group at CICFC
location:
Paul McDermott Physical Education - Building A
        Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling
MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Mindfulness Arts and Crafts Corner
description: A calming place to slow down, breathe, and express yourself through soothing arts and crafts.

name: Podcasting 102: Audio Mixing
description: Join David Ramos Candelas, multimedia and design specialist, to learn how to polish your podcast using audio editing tools like de-noisers and EQ. During this one-hour workshop, you will explore Lied Library's multimedia resources and practice audio mixing.

name: BEST Career Expo
description: The UNLV Lee Business School, College of Engineering, and the School of Sciences invites you to the BEST Career Expo—an exclusive networking event designed for business, engineering, tech, and science students. This is your opportunity to connect with unique employers hiring for roles in these fields the night before the UNLV-wide Career Fair.

name: Hospitality Career Fair Spring 2026
description: Connect with the future of hospitality at the UNLV Hospitality Career Fair, the premier recruiting event for students and employers in the industry. Meet with 70+ leading hospitality employers representing a wide range of sectors, including hotels, restaurants, gaming, golf, and meetings and events.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: UNLV Quiz Bowl
description: Join us for UNLV's Quiz Bowl, where teams will compete to answer questions about history, literature, science, fine arts, current events, popular culture, sports, and more. Teams of 3-6 students, alumni, and faculty/staff members can sign up by Feb. 15. The winning team will receive a prize. Teams must have at least one student. Food and drinks will be provided.

name: February Alumni Mixer at the Hospitality Career Fair
description: Calling all hospitality alumni! Get ready to mix, mingle, and reconnect at the Hospitality Alumni Monthly Mixer. Join us for an evening of career and Rebel reconnections, starting with the Hospitality Career Fair from 4 - 6 p.m. Meet industry leaders, explore new opportunities, and see what’s next in the world of hospitality.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
UNLV Career & Internship Fair Spring 2026
location:
Thomas and Mack Center
        Thomas & Mack Concourse
description: UNLV students and alumni are invited to attend the Career & Internship Fair, where you'll meet employers from a wide range of industries who are hiring for full-time, part-time, and internship positions.

description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Moth Ball, an installation of a single risograph print in the Museum’s Window Gallery.

name: General Study Abroad Scholarship Workshop
description: Drop by our Study Abroad Scholarship Workshop to find funding and get quick tips on applying. We'll also review and revise your essays and applications. It’s an easy way to boost your chances for free money to go abroad.

name: Art Exhibition: El camino se traza al andar (The Path is Traced by Walking)
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center

description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking), an exhibition of artworks by the Los Angeles-based interdisciplinary artist Jackie Amézquita.

Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center

Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Meet a First Gen Mentor
description: Meet The Intersection's First Gen Mentors; feel free to ask them any questions you might have as a student and they will share with you all that they have learned. We look forward to seeing you.

name: Beginners' Idea Bridge Workshop Series: Viability of a Patent
description: This workshop will teach you about inventions, parts of a patent, and how to search for patents in the USPTOs database. You will take part in learning activities to increase your understanding of patent topics.

name: Keep Calm and Carry Yarn
description: Yarn crafters and yarn-craft-curious folks are invited to join a Keep Calm and Carry Yarn gathering in the Lied Library lobby. We invite thread and embroidery floss crafters to join us! Bring your current work in progress or stop by for inspiration and conversation.

name: Pre-Med Veterans Mentoring Session
description: Veteran students and representatives from the Office of Community Affairs at the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV will be available to mentor pre-medicine student veterans from UNLV, NSC, CSN, and UNR who are interested in learning about general admissions for UNLV School of Medicine. Appointments are required.

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Graduate College: Ph.D. Prospects: Faculty or Non-Academic Career? How to Decide What's Right for You
description: Deciding between a faculty career and non-academic opportunities is a complex and challenging choice for Ph.D. students, with the potential for long-term impact. This workshop will help you reflect on your strengths, values, and long-term goals to determine the path that aligns best with your aspirations. Panelists will dig into the realities of both academic and non-academic careers and discuss motivations for exploring each path.

name: Money Talks–Tax Returns: Easier Than Your Finals!
description: Forget all-nighters and exam stress—this tax workshop is truly Easier Than Your Finals! We'll break down the federal tax return process into simple, repeatable steps using the essential Form 1040. You will master your W-2 and your 1098-T tuition forms, figure out your dependency status, and calculate your adjusted gross income, standard deduction, and taxable income. Use practical examples to walk through the exact steps for calculating your biggest possible refund, so you can get your cash back faster and file with absolute confidence.

name: Recovery Curious
location:
Student Services Complex - Building A
        CAPS Suite 143
description: Are you curious about substance use recovery or ways to reduce use? Join us virtually or in-person to share questions or receive support around the topic of substance use and recovery.

name: 3rd Annual Art & Anthropology Exhibit
location:
John S. Wright Hall - Building B
        114
description: Students and faculty exhibit of artwork relating to themes of anthropology. Awards are also given to the top three artworks!

name: Curiosity Compass: Mapping Your Path to Meaningful Research
description: This interactive workshop invites students to explore their natural curiosity and understand how it connects to academic disciplines. You will take a curiosity assessment to identify your personal curiosity style, reflect on information you naturally gravitate toward in everyday life, and discover how these interests align with scholarly fields.

name: Baccarat: Inside Chinese Gambling Culture
location:
Stan Fulton Building - International Gaming Institute
        KONAMI Gaming Laboratory
description: Baccarat generates billions in Las Vegas every year, yet most people walk right past the tables without understanding the cultural rituals behind the games. This hands-on lecture explores Chinese gambling culture at the baccarat table. You'll learn the art of card squeezing, discover the meaning behind the chants during play, and experience the cultural practices that make baccarat the game of choice for Chinese gamblers worldwide. No prior gambling knowledge is required.

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Importance of Participating in Your Employer's Retirement Plan - Webinar
description: Your employer’s retirement plan is an essential component of your future financial security. It is important that you understand how your plan works and what benefits you will receive. Just as you keep track of the money that you put in your bank or any other financial institution, it is in your best interest to understand and monitor your retirement plan and your benefits.

name: Meet with a TIAA Representative on Campus
description: Meet with a TIAA financial consultant to help create a financial plan for your future. Discuss investments, saving and withdrawing from your plan when you retire. Registration is required.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Historic Westside Bus Tour
description: The Historic Westside Bus Tour is a guided experiential learning opportunity highlighting the history, culture, and legacy of Black communities in Las Vegas’ Historic Westside. This event emphasizes learning beyond the classroom. Space is limited and advance registration is required. Open to everyone. More details and RSVP

Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Center Gallery

location:

description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Moth Ball, an installation of a single risograph print in the Museum’s Window Gallery.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: Art Exhibition: El camino se traza al andar (The Path is Traced by Walking)
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art West Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking), an exhibition of artworks by the Los Angeles-based interdisciplinary artist Jackie Amézquita.

name: QGIS Workshop Series: GIS Data Management
location:
Lied Library
        Amargosa Room or Online
description: This workshop equips participants with practical skills for creating, editing, and managing geospatial data. Participants will explore common geospatial data types and sources while learning best practices for organizing and maintaining a well-structured GIS database. This workshop is part of the Rebel+ Libraries Workshop Series.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Success Series: Test Taking Strategies/Note taking
description: This workshop is designed to help students strengthen note-taking and test-taking skills. Students will learn practical strategies to improve organization, comprehension, and exam performance in an engaging online format.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Faculty and Academic Advisor Mixer
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Faculty Lounge (Room 235)
description: Please join us for a faculty and academic advisor mixer hosted by the Teaching and Learning Commons. Supporting today’s students is a team effort. When faculty and advisors connect, student success follows. Join us to meet, mingle, and strengthen the partnerships that make a difference. By connecting the people who guide students every day, we build a consistent, coordinated network of support, one in which students feel seen and supported. Lunch will be provided!

name: Narcan Training
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        1502 - Health Education Room
description: Join us for a Narcan Training focused on opioid overdose awareness and response. Participants will learn what opioids are, how to recognize the signs of an overdose, and the steps to safely intervene and help save a life if ever needed. This training centers on harm reduction, community care, and building confidence to respond in an emergency. Attendees will receive free Narcan, a CPR mask, and a first aid kit to take home. **Chick-fil-A will be provided while supplies last on a first-come, first-served basis.**

name: Global Thursday: Culture and Conversation
description: Learn about different cultures and share your own, enjoy international snacks, and make new friends every Global Thursday. All UNLV students are welcome!

name: Democracy Defense Project with Former Governor Sandoval, Former Assembly Speaker Perkins, and Lorena Portillo Clark County Registrar of Voters
description: As a nonpartisan, nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening trust in elections — particularly in battleground states like Nevada — the DDP is co‐chaired here in Nevada by former Governor Brian Sandoval and former Assembly Speaker Richard Perkins. Both are eager to engage with students on the importance of free, fair, and secure elections, and to discuss why voters can have confidence in Nevada’s electoral process.

name: Nursing: CRNA Open House
location: Clinical Simulation Center of Las Vegas, 1001 Shadow Lane
description: UNLV Nursing welcomes prospective students, healthcare professionals, community leaders, and the general public to learn more about the Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist (CRNA) Program, tour the simulation lab, and connect with program directors, current students, and leadership from the Nevada Association of Nurse Anesthetists (NANA). RSVP

name: Virtual Research Seminar: Increasing the Visibility of Your Research
description: The office of research invites you to their upcoming virtual research seminar on how to increase the visibility of your research. Earn 1 CME Credit or a certificate of attendance.

name: Lee Grad Essentials: Canva 101
description: Hosted by UNLV AMA students, this interactive Canva 101 workshop will introduce participants to the fundamentals of Canva as a tool for creating professional, eye-catching designs. Attendees will learn how to navigate the Canva platform, use templates effectively, apply basic design principles, and create graphics for social media, presentations, and marketing materials.

name: Virtual Graduate:  Master of Science in Accounting Information Session
description: Are you looking to advance your career in accounting and prepare for the CPA exam? Join us to learn about the Master of Science in Accounting program at UNLV. Whether you're a current accounting professional, seeking to deepen your expertise, or considering a career switch, this program is designed to meet your needs. Our faculty and staff will discuss the program's structure, curriculum, and how it can help you prepare for the CPA exam.

name: UNLV Wind Orchestra: The Pursuit of Happiness
description: The UNLV Wind Orchestra will conclude the 2025-26 UNLV Division of Wind Band Studies Concert Series with a powerful and forward-looking program titled The Pursuit of Happiness.

name: UNLV Wind Orchestra: Grad Rebel Appreciation Night
description: The UNLV Wind Orchestra has received international acclaim for its fresh and creative approach to music making. Performing contemporary repertoire in addition to classical masterworks, the Wind Orchestra at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas has been responsible for commissioning and premiering numerous significant new works by America’s finest young contemporary composers including: Eric Whitacre, Wendell Yuponce, Jonathon Newman, Steven Bryant, Jim Bonney, Corey Hirsch, Paul Seitz, Nathan Tanouye, Kenneth Froelich and Wataru Hokoyama.

name: Last Day To Completely Withdraw From All Classes and Receive a 50% Refund if Your Account Is Paid in Full
UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Meet with a TIAA Representative on Campus
description: Meet with a TIAA financial consultant to help create a financial plan for your future. Discuss investments, saving and withdrawing from your plan when you retire. Registration is required.

name: Health Sciences Research Symposium
description: The UNLV Health Sciences Research Symposium brings together researchers, clinicians, educators, and students to showcase emerging evidence, exchange ideas, and build meaningful research connections. The symposium’s mission is to advance discovery, innovation, and collaboration across the full spectrum of health-related disciplines. By fostering cross-disciplinary dialogue with researchers from UNLV and beyond, the symposium helps accelerate the translation of knowledge from basic science to clinical practice.

name: Catholic Charities Food Serving
description: Join UNLVolunteers for a meaningful morning of service with Catholic Charities as we help provide 300–500 meals to individuals experiencing homelessness and food insecurity in our community. Volunteers will assist with serving food and water, cleaning tables, and breaking down used plates and trays. All volunteers should be comfortable standing for up to two hours and be willing to help wherever needed. The service event will take place from 8:30 a.m. to 12 p.m. Please wear long pants, closed-toe shoes, and a shirt with sleeves (no crop tops or tank tops).

name: Rebel Red Friday
description: Non-local students and families who are searching for a college are invited to join us for an exclusive day at UNLV! Immerse yourself in our campus life, hear directly from current students, explore our diverse colleges, and discover our housing and dining options. You'll even have the opportunity to fill out an application on the spot.

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: The Right to Education in the 21st Century: Challenges and Opportunities
description: Education is recognized under international law as a fundamental human right and a pathway for the realization of other rights. Yet millions worldwide continue to face barriers to access and quality in education. In this online conversation, the UN Special Rapporteur on the Right to Education, Farida Shaheed, will explore the global state of education today: who has access, who is left behind, and what governments and institutions are obligated to do under international human rights law.

name: Art Exhibition: El camino se traza al andar (The Path is Traced by Walking)
location:

name: REBELPerform Evaluation Training
description: All administrative and classified staff complete their performance evaluations on REBELPerform. This training will provide an overview of the REBELPerform evaluation process. You will learn how to access the evaluation, navigate through the form, add attachments to the evaluation, download evaluations as a PDF, print the evaluations, and more. There will also be a live demonstration and an opportunity to ask questions. This training also serves as a refresher course for those who need additional guidance on how to complete the performance evaluation.

name: Pima Medical Institute: Graduation Ceremony (Certificate Programs)
description: The Las Vegas Campus of the Pima Medical Institute hosts its commencement exercise.

name: Junk Journaling
description: Join us in the makerspace for junk journaling, a creative and therapeutic practice that involves using scraps, discarded materials, or "junk" to create a journal. Learn about different way you can build and have fun with your journal. Materials will be provided. No orientation is required to attend this workshop, but if you haven't done one yet we encourage you to attend one to learn more about our space and become a registered user.

name: Let's Talk About It: Coping with Social Anxiety
description: Avoiding presentations? Struggling to make friends? Feel awkward in social situations? Attend this virtual workshop to learn ways to cope with social anxiety!

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: UNLV Global Success Series: The International Student Financial 'Cheat Sheet': How to Master U.S. Credit and Housing
description: Join us for "The International Student Financial 'Cheat Sheet': How to Master U.S. Credit and Housing." Do you want to decode the U.S. financial system quickly? This workshop is your shortcut. We reveal the hidden mechanics behind American banking, credit scores, and apartment hunting. You will leave with practical scripts for negotiating with landlords and a clear strategy to build your financial reputation. Do not just translate the language. Translate the system.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: "Natural Psychological Kinds - An Integrative Proposal"
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room 122
description: Jasmin Özel, Minnesota Center for Philosophy of Science, University of Minnesota, Twin Cities —The homeostatic property cluster framework for natural kinds has proven useful across various scientific contexts, particularly those requiring multiple levels of explanation.

name: Pima Medical Institute: Graduation Ceremony (Degree Programs)
description: The Las Vegas Campus of the Pima Medical Institute hosts its commencement exercise.

name: Alexander Felix Presents Master of Music Percussion Recital
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Percussion Studio, room HFA 235 (second floor)
description: This recital is for partial completion of Felix's Master of Music degree. Other performers include close friends and colleagues Finn Caoile and Matthew Hall on piano as well as Nicholas Berkey and Austin Russell assisting on percussion with Trevis Phillips featured on bass guitar. Felix will be performing works of varying styles and time periods for modern percussion solo and chamber music including a work for solo marimba composed by UNLV's very own Dr. Dean Gronemeier.

name: Oboe and Harpsichord Recital
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Andrea Ridilla (oboe, Miami University), Richard Kravchak (oboe, College of Southern Nevada), Giacomo Benedetti (harpsichord, Basilica of San Miniato al Monte and the Church of Santa Felicità in Florence, Italy), and Andrew Smith (cello, UNLV) present a concert of baroque music. Repertoire includes pieces by Handel, Boni, Vivaldi, Scarlatti, and more.

name: UNLV Dance: Universal Motions
description: Universal Motions features choreography by the UNLV Dance faculty, including a reconstruction of a work by 20th century American choreographer, Erick Hawkins.

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
UNLV Double Reed Day
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall, Room 159 and 160
description: Please join UNLV School of Music Double Reed faculty Richard Kravchak, Ph.D., and Liz Valvano, D.M.A, as we present our first double reed day! This free event is open to all double reed, oboe and bassoon, musicians and music educators in Las Vegas. We will have a double reed ensemble for all musicians to perform, as well as vendors exhibiting double reed items for purchase, recitals featuring current and UNLV alumni, and masterclasses with guest artists including oboist Andrea Ridilla from Miami University. We hope to see you here!

name: West Coast Consortium Las Vegas Law School Fair
description: Partnering with the West Coast Consortium of Private Law Schools (WCC), the William S. Boyd School of Law invites you to the WCC Las Vegas Law School Fair. Meet admission officers and representatives from more than 20 law schools and the Law School Admission Council (LSAC). Registration is free and will remain open until Friday, February 27 at 12 p.m. Walk-ins are also welcome.

The College of Education graduate student and alumni community is invited to a family-friendly puptactular event to create connections across disciplines, celebrate our students and alumni, and fawn over our four-legged friends. Alumni, graduate students, faculty, staff, and supporters* are invited to bring their families and puppies on Saturday, February 28 to enjoy pizza and networking with fellow alumni. *Minors must be accompanied with a parent or guardian; no more than 2 children per chaperone.

description: Come and join the SIHS Alumni Chapter for a basketball tailgate with a special offer for our Nutrition Sciences alumni in celebration of the program's 25th anniversary. We'll be joining the UNLV Alumni Association tailgate for the men’s basketball game as our Runnin' Rebels take on UNR on Feb. 28. The tailgate is located in the Strip View Pavilion inside Thomas & Mack Center.

location:

1861 N. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd. Las Vegas, NV 89106

location:
Thomas and Mack Center
        Strip View Pavilion
description: Basketball season is officially underway! Join your Rebels Forever family and support the Runnin' Rebels as they take the court against their leading rival the UNR Wolfpack. Join your fellow Rebels for food, drinks, entertainment, and fan giveaways in the Strip View Pavilion, located at the Thomas & Mack Center. This pregame event is in partnership with UNLV Athletics. To ensure the best fan experience possible, please register in advance.

Last Day for Undergraduates to Apply for May 2026 Degree Conferral
Nursing: Final Day to Notify Intent to Return
Family Swim at SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during this time. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Gratitude Brief Practice
description: Research has shown practicing gratitude leads to a number of mental, physical, and relational benefits; yet it is so easy to be swept up in the seemingly relentless demands of a busy life and feel you don’t have the time to take a moment to consider and appreciate the things you are grateful for. Give yourself the gift of this 10-minute gratitude practice and prioritize your self-care and well-being. Register here for our 10 to 15-minute Gratitude practice!

name: Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit
description: Thomas Mann (1875 – 1955) was a German novelist, social critic, and Nobel laureate. The “Democracy Will Win” exhibit explores his life and political evolution, and connects history to the present through a variety of media featuring voices from politics, pop culture, and literature.

description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: #SupportSquad: The Real Ones
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        235 (TLC Faculty Lounge)
description: If humans are social creatures, then, why is it so hard to make friends? Because being human is hard. Despite what we have been taught or maybe even experienced, we do best when in community with one another. Let’s talk about how to make real connections with each other.

name: Success Series: Effective Communication #2
description: In this Success Series session, Lean-In to Effective Communication, students will explore the importance of clear, intentional communication and how it supports success throughout the semester. We’ll also discuss how to clearly express your needs, navigate different communication styles, and highlight helpful ASC resources to support you along the way. The UNLV Writing Center will join to provide general tips on clarity in writing and professional email communication, helping you communicate confidently across campus.

700 College Dr.
Henderson, NV 89002
description: Join us for a fun game day and drop-in advising! Enjoy games, meet fellow students, and get quick, no-appointment-needed advising to support your transfer journey.

name: Teaching @ UNLV – WebCampus Workshop Series: Providing Feedback – Mid-Semester
description: The focus of this workshop is on using WebCampus (Canvas) to conduct a meaningful mid-semester review that enhances teaching and learning across all modalities—whether face-to-face, online, or hybrid. With an emphasis on student success, participants will explore how to assess current learning, provide timely and constructive feedback, and make data-informed instructional adjustments using tools within WebCampus.

name: Leadership Link Up : Spongebob Squarepants - When Plankton Pushes Your Buttons
description: Dealing with a plankton in your life? Whether it’s a team member stirring the pot or someone pushing your buttons, this session is all about handling conflict with grace. Participants will:​ Identify personal emotional triggers and their influence on leadership behavior.​ Explore techniques for maintaining emotional regulation and boundaries during interpersonal conflict.​ Practice conflict resolution strategies that align with values, compassion, and team cohesion.​

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Intuitive Eating Group at CICFC
location:
Paul McDermott Physical Education - Building A
        Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling
MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Readings with Young German Writers
description: Hear readings and take part in an informal discussion with two up-and-coming young German writers, Louise Kenn and Mücahit Türk, as they launch their month‑long literary journey across the United States at UNLV. They will offer a distinctive cross‑cultural glimpse into contemporary German literature. The event takes place in the framework of the current Thomas Mann "Democracy Will Win" Exhibit presented in cooperation with the Thomas Mann House.

name: The Price of Democracy: The Revolutionary Power of Taxation in American History
description: As part of the Brookings Scholar Lecture Series, Brookings Mountain West presents a lecture titled, "The Price of Democracy: The Revolutionary Power of Taxation in American History" by Brookings senior fellow in governance studies, Vanessa Williamson.

Student Recreation & Wellness Center

description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: Sunset Yoga – Women HerStory Month Kickoff
description: Kick off Women HerStory Month with an evening of movement and community. The program will feature yoga, ReggaeTONE Pilates, a sound bath, and a photo booth in an outdoor setting designed to promote wellness and connection. A charcuterie spread provided by Aramark will be available. Participants are encouraged to bring their own mat; a limited number of mats will be available. In partnership with Girl Gains UNLV, UNLV Rebel Rec, Vegas Girl Events, and New Era Performance & Recovery. Open to all members of the campus community.

description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. Students will learn how to use Google’s built-in tools to streamline their writing process.

name: REB Event
description: Come celebrate the spring semester with REB at this event with free food, activities, and more!

name: In-Person: MBA NSHE Lunch and Learn Session
description: Please join us to review the UNLV MBA program (hybrid evening and online) tailored for NSHE employees. The session will be held in room BEH 239. An overview of the UNLV MBA program (hybrid evening and online) tailored for NSHE employees. We'll cover program structure, application tips, GMAT/GRE waiver options, and how to use your NSHE Grant-in-Aid benefits. There will also be time for Q&A. Lunch provided!

name: UNLV Women's Council Spring Gathering
description: The UNLV Women’s Council invites faculty and staff to join us for the Women’s Council Spring Gathering (formerly the Annual Membership Meeting). Enjoy lunch while connecting with colleagues from across campus and learning more about the council’s advocacy efforts, programs, and exciting upcoming events. The UNLV Women’s Council brings together a diverse community of faculty and staff who are passionate about supporting UNLV and its students. This gathering is open to both current and prospective members—we’d love to see you there!

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Speakeasy Series: Speak Your LOVE
description: Come write a love letter, make a friendship bracelet, and create an affirmation jar for yourself or others. Enjoy matcha and snacks! Join us for a drop-in space to eat and share your story. Meet with other students and CAPS staff to speak your story, give and get support, and engage in activities for reflection and growth.

name: Writing Essentials Wednesdays
description: This semester, join the Writing Center on the first Wednesday of each month at 1 p.m. to strengthen your writing habits and skills. This spring, we'll be focusing on starting out strong. Have a paper due soon that you haven't yet begun? Were you just assigned an essay for class? Attend this workshop to learn strategies to set yourself up for success. We'll touch on topics such as turning your assignment prompt into a helpful checklist; organizing your ideas; and citing your sources.

name: Thesis Defense: Jeffrey Harkness

Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room 226

name: Communication Skills Workshop
description: This virtual four-session workshop will provide students with a new set of communication skills each session.

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Virtual: Alternative Route to Licensure (Traditional) Information Session
description: This information session is specifically for our traditional ARL program and does not cover the Accelerated Graduate Apprenticeship program. For details on the next cohort cycle of the Graduate Apprenticeship program (formerly known as A-ARL), please visit the program's program's website.

name: Community Talk #2: Trauma Heals in the Presence of Caring Others: Indigenous Relationality and Collective Pathways to Wellbeing
description: Join the UNLV College of Education and guest speaker, Emma Elliott, for the next installment of the Dunn Community Talk Series.

name: Intro To Triathlon - Running
description: Join us in preparation for the Triathlon Spring event on April 4. During this class we will guide you through different exercises, techniques and strategies for preparing for the running leg of a triathlon. We invite anyone regardless of running level or triathlon experience to join us in this free event.

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. This session focuses on using Google Docs to write, revise, and submit academic assignments more efficiently. name: Emotional Intelligence
description: Overview/ Description: Implementing discipline over our emotional life is difficult and requires lifelong practice. Being able to choose to act or respond in a particular manner is preferable to re-acting on a consistent basis, yet it is difficult. Enhancing emotional intelligence improves our ability to master our emotional functioning. High emotional intelligence can improve our communications with all others, enhance our perception of satisfaction with ourselves and our lives and increase our productiveness overall.

name: Teaching @ UNLV – WebCampus Workshop Series: Providing Feedback – Mid-Semester
description: The focus of this workshop is on using WebCampus (Canvas) to conduct a meaningful mid-semester review that enhances teaching and learning across all modalities—whether face-to-face, online, or hybrid. With an emphasis on student success, participants will explore how to assess current learning, provide timely and constructive feedback, and make data-informed instructional adjustments using tools within WebCampus.

name: Beyond the Deficit: Relationships and Rigor: Asset-Based Teaching: An Introduction

Celebrate the beauty of nature and women artists who have used floral motifs in their work while creating your own unique bookmark. This calming activity encourages creativity, reduces stress, and connects you to the present moment, all supporting your emotional and creative wellness.

name: Global Thursday: Language Exchange
description: Interested in learning or practicing a new language? Do you love exploring language-culture connections? Join us every second Global Thursday for the Language Exchange Program (LEP) where students can explore languages such as Chinese, German, Vietnamese, Persian, Japanese, and more in a relaxed environment. No prior study required!

name: University Forum Lecture: Punk Is a Moving Target
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: Mimi Thi Nguyen, professor of women's, gender, and sexuality studies at Dartmouth College, will discuss punk as a moving target with forms, structures, aesthetics, and networks of feelings that are not stable or the same in any time or place. Nguyen makes sense of living within the spaces where she met punk's promise -- through war, forced removal, crimes of private property and public disorder, and chance encounters with friends and strangers.

Mindfulness Brief Practice
description: It can be difficult to focus on the present and not feel swept up in our 'to do' list for the future or what has happened in the past. However, research has shown many benefits to being present and paying attention to the present moment without judgement (i.e. being mindful). When you are aware, it allows you to choose how you want to respond to life’s experiences – both the positives and the stressful. Of course, like most skills worth growing, learning to be mindful takes practice.

name: Responsible Conduct of Research Training
description: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) training is an essential component to make certain that the research activities of UNLV faculty, staff, and students are conducted with integrity and in compliance with regulatory guidance. RCR is defined by The National Institutes of Health (NIH) as the practice of scientific investigation with integrity. It involves the awareness and application of established professional norms and ethical principles in the performance of all activities related to scientific research.

name: "Does Cognitive Science Show That Digital Minds Are Possible?"
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room 122
description: Carl Sachs, School of Humanities, Marymount University—One widespread argument for the feasibility of digital minds holds that digital minds are possible because biological cognition is computational and computational processes are substrate-neutral. If biological cognition is essentially computational, and computations are substrate-neutral, then the same or similar computational processes could be implemented by digital computers. In short, digital minds are possible just because biological minds are themselves computational.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building B

description: Center for Democratic Culture sponsors a lecture by Richard Shusterman at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. Shusterman is the Dorothy F. Schmidt Eminent Scholar in the Humanities. Educated at Jerusalem and Oxford, he was chair of the Temple University Philosophy Department before coming to FAU in 2005. He has held academic appointments in Paris, Berlin, and Hiroshima and was awarded senior research Fulbright and NEH fellowships.

description: These virtual information sessions are for individuals who are starting to consider medical school as a graduate degree option and career path. We welcome high school seniors, community college and university students, or other individuals just beginning to research medical school and/or beginning to plan for their application. We will offer an overview of the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV, resources to help consider medical school, details regarding application, and a Q&A at the end of the session.

name: Graduate: Virtual Master of Science in Quantitative Finance Information Session
description: Interested in earning a Master of Science in Quantitative Finance at UNLV? Join us to learn how you can complete this rigorous and rewarding degree in just one year. Our faculty and staff will provide insights into the program's structure, curriculum, and the unique opportunities it offers. Discover how this accelerated degree can help you achieve your career goals in the finance industry.

location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        Room 101
description: Niloufar Talebi’s new book, a bilingual edition of Ahmad Shamlou, one of Iran’s most influential 20th-century poets, showcases her skill and dedication as a translator and cultural worker. In Elegies of the Earth, published to mark the poet’s centennial, Talebi brings Shamlou’s revolutionary voice of resistance and modernity to English readers.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Progressive Muscle Relaxation
description: Come and join us in this practice of relaxation as we slowly work our way across various muscle groups to decrease tension and stress. Progressive Muscle Relaxation or PMR, is a relaxation practice developed in the early 1900’s and has repeatedly proven to help with relaxation of the mind and body. Through increasing awareness of what tension feels like, and then using techniques to help your muscles and body relax, the mind follows. PMR has also been shown to support pain relief, better sleep, and other mind-body benefits.

Plus, we'll show you exactly what to do if you ever need to appeal. Get the info you need to stay on track and keep your aid flowing!

name: Graduate College: Thesis and Dissertation Process: Steps for Formatting Review and Common Mistakes
description: As students complete a thesis, dissertation, or doctoral project, they often focus on the content. Formatting is usually an afterthought, if a thought at all! Yet formatting is critical because your submission cannot be accepted or approved unless formatted properly. Thinking about formatting from the outset can save valuable time, mental bandwidth, and emotional energy near the submission deadline. By attending this workshop, you will learn insider tips and tricks from UNLV’s T/D Formatting Manager, Dr. Jennifer Stevens.

name: Narcan Training
description: Join us for a Narcan Training focused on opioid overdose awareness and response. Participants will learn what opioids are, how to recognize the signs of an overdose, and the steps to safely intervene and help save a life if ever needed. This training centers on harm reduction, community care, and building confidence to respond in an emergency. Attendees will receive free Narcan, a CPR mask, and a first aid kit to take home. Chick-fil-A will be provided while supplies last on a first-come, first-served basis.

Join us at Chicken N Pickle for an evening of connection, camaraderie, and community spirit as we rally together in support of the students and the future of the UNLV Harrah College of Hospitality.

This year’s theme “The Worker” is a contemporary and immediate snapshot of the American workforce—a compelling and fascinating look at work, jobs, and the people who do them. Join NCT as we bring you six world premiere short plays written for Las Vegas by some of the most thrilling voices in American theatre. Shorts is made possible by the David and Stephanie Vondrak Short Play Festival Fund.

location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Victoria Williams presents a night of chamber music featuring the clarinet. Each work explores a contrasting era and style, showcasing the clarinet's wide range of musical range and versatility, ranging from the classical to the modern and contemporary.

name: R. Frank & D. Schuman Astronomy Lecture Series: Black Holes at Work
description: Black Holes at Work: How Invisible Monsters Shape Galaxies Travis Fischer, Space Telescope Science Institute in Baltimore Black holes are often portrayed as cosmic vacuum cleaners,but in reality they are powerful engines that shape the galaxies around them. In this talk, Fischer explains what black holes really are, how astronomers detect them despite their invisibility, and how observations with the James Webb Space Telescope allow us to see how black holes drive winds, shocks, and change their host galaxies. Hosted by the UNLV Physics and Astronomy Department.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Responsible Conduct of Research Training
description: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) training is an essential component to make certain that the research activities of UNLV faculty, staff, and students are conducted with integrity and in compliance with regulatory guidance. RCR is defined by The National Institutes of Health (NIH) as the practice of scientific investigation with integrity. It involves the awareness and application of established professional norms and ethical principles in the performance of all activities related to scientific research.

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

Kathleen Krach, Advisory Committee Co-Chair Katherine Lee, Advisory Committee Member LeAnne Salazar Montoya, Graduate College Representative

name: HSI Resource Hub March Webinar: SomosSTEM - A Community-Based, Culturally Engaging STEM Learning Environment at a Rural Hispanic-Serving Institution
description: SomosSTEM is an integrated, community-based, and culturally engaging STEM learning environment at New Mexico Highlands University (NMHU), a public comprehensive Hispanic Serving Institution in Las Vegas, New Mexico. SomosSTEM weaves place-based course-based undergraduate research experiences (CUREs), community internships, near-peer co-designed curriculum (intern-built CUREs), faculty development in culturally responsive mentoring, and sustained partnerships with local agencies into a coherent, longitudinal pathway to promote undergraduate persistence in the life sciences.

name: Chinese Ink Painting
description: This 6-week course introduces the fundamentals of traditional Chinese ink painting. Students will explore brush techniques, ink control, and composition while learning to paint classic subjects such as bamboo, flowers, birds, and simple landscapes. Emphasis is placed on expressive brushwork, balance, and the philosophy behind ink painting. Open to beginners and all levels, no prior experience required.

This trip includes transportation, food, instruction, and all necessary equipment.

name: Cutting Through the Clutter
description: Overview/Description: Most people have no idea how much their clutter affects them. It can affect their productivity, their self-esteem, their social life and even their weight! People who live with excess clutter say they can’t find the energy to begin to clear it. They constantly feel tired and overwhelmed. When surrounded by clutter, it is impossible to focus or have clarity about life. In this workshop you will learn that clutter is not the result of sloppiness, laziness or incompetence but several surmountable obstacles.

name: Calligraphy Workshop
description: In this six-week workshop, students will be introduced to all the essential materials, techniques, and foundational skills needed for Chinese calligraphy. You will learn how to use traditional inkstones and how to maintain your brushes properly. Through guided demonstrations and individual practice, students will develop confidence and control over their brushwork. By the end of the workshop, students will be able to create their own finished calligraphy piece.

name: Guided Breathing Practice
description: Revitalize your mind and body in just 15 minutes with alternate nostril breathing. Whether you need an energy boost or a grounding practice to center yourself, this session has you covered.

name: Gratitude Brief Practice

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: American Red Cross Lifeguarding Certification Course
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        1210 Wet Classroom
description: The American Red Cross Lifeguarding classes are designed to teach you not only the basics in water safety but also the proper protocol when dealing with any emergencies around water. The Red Cross blends both traditional lecture style and crucial hands-on training in order to teach you effective techniques to deliver prompt care to those in need. After successful completion of the course, participants will be certified in lifeguarding, CPR for the professional rescuer, AED, and first aid.

Guided Meditation Practice
description: Modern life often keeps us in a constant state of motion—thinking, planning, and responding. Meditation offers a brief but meaningful opportunity to pause, settle the body, and create mental space, even in the middle of a busy day. This Guided Meditation Practice is designed to support relaxation, focus, and emotional balance by gently bringing attention back to the present moment. Through intentional breathing and awareness, participants are invited to release tension, calm the nervous system, and cultivate a sense of steadiness and ease.

name: Canoe Journey to Emerald Cove
description: Go paddling down the Colorado River with Outdoor Adventures! This trip features a 4-mile round-trip canoe journey on the stunning green waters of the Colorado River as we make our way to the famous Emerald Cove. This beginner-friendly paddle is great for both new and experienced canoers—no prior experience required. The trip includes transportation, equipment, instruction, and food.

description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! description: Knowing Your Calories: Reading Food Labels Made Easy, will focus on helping individuals understand nutrition labels, including serving sizes, calories, and key nutrients. The goal is to equip the audience with practical skills to make informed and healthier food choices.

location:

description: The My Professional Origin Story series, hosted by the UNLV Graduate College, highlights the professional journeys of UNLV faculty and administrators. Origin stories matter. Understanding how people came to be where they are can give us insight into where we might be headed or how our own journeys unfold. Appetizers and light refreshments will be provided for in-person attendees.

name: Success Series: Money Matters #1
description: This workshop will cover essential financial topics, including key financial terms, saving money, financing your education, setting financial goals, budgeting, making smart financial decisions after graduation, investing, and planning for major purchases. Equip yourself with the knowledge and skills to confidently navigate your financial journey from student life to your future career. For more information, visit the Student Success Center Coaching page.

description: Celebrate Nowruz and the Persian New Year as we honor renewal, culture, and community. This program recognizes the arrival of spring and the beginning of a new year through cultural celebration and shared connection. Dinner will be provided. Open to all members of the campus community. More information.

description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Virtual LEE Business Grad School 101 Information Session
description: Find out more about the graduate degree options that the LEE Business School offers. Every student is different. They have varied goals, backgrounds, interests, and these all factor into the degrees they choose. We invite you to explore our programs to find the one that works for you. We provide more than a graduate degree; we provide a graduate student experience. Find out how to be part of the experience during this session.

name: RSI Workshop Series: Direct Instruction – Improving Interaction & Engagement Within Your Courses
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Direct Instruction. This training will focus on the following outcomes:

name: Perspectives in the Information Ecosystem
description: Whose voices are heard, and whose are not? When is a good time to consult an expert and when is a good time to consult a friend? At its core, research means seeing what other people have to say about your research question and figuring out what that means to you. Learning from others helps you understand your question better. This workshop will explore whose perspectives you can find throughout the information ecosystem, and how to know whose perspective to seek out for a given question.

name: Administrative Faculty March General Meeting
description: Regular monthly meeting to discuss issues of importance to administrative faculty. All administrative faculty are invited to attend online via Zoom. They can also submit items for the agenda anonymously via our New Business for AFC form.

name: Mindfulness Arts and Crafts Corner
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 1502 (HED ROOM)
description: A calming place to slow down, breathe, and express yourself through soothing arts and crafts.

name: We Need To Talk About AI
description: Artificial intelligence is reshaping how we teach, learn, create, and govern – but at what cost, and under whose rules? This conversation will examine AI’s expanding role in higher education through the lenses of literacy and access, intellectual property and copyright, environmental impact, and the evolving ethical tensions that define this moment. Join us for a candid, interdisciplinary conversation about how we can embrace AI’s possibilities while thoughtfully addressing its risks.

name: Student Veteran and First Generation Study Night in the Library
description: Calling all student veterans and first-generation students! Need help with coursework or looking for a supportive study space? Join us for student veteran and first-generation study night in the library! Drop-in any time during the event to connect with fellow student veterans and first-gen students, access libraries resources, and receive academic support from the libraries. Snacks will be provided. Sponsored by: UNLV Libraries, Rebel Vets, and UNLV First Gen Club.

name: Club Management Association of America - Mock Interviews
description: Practice your Interview Skills with UNLV CMAA! This is a great opportunity to get better and test your interview skills. The mock interview event hosted by UNLV Club Management Association of America helps you practice in a low-pressure setting while receiving valuable feedback. It improves communication skills, boosts confidence, and prepares you for tough questions. By identifying areas for improvement, it enhances professionalism and increases your chances of success in real interviews.

name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
description: Come and join us in this practice of relaxation as we slowly work our way across various muscle groups to decrease tension and stress. Progressive Muscle Relaxation or PMR, is a relaxation practice developed in the early 1900’s and has repeatedly proven to help with relaxation of the mind and body. Through increasing awareness of what tension feels like, and then using techniques to help your muscles and body relax, the mind follows. PMR has also been shown to support pain relief, better sleep, and other mind-body benefits.

name: Money Talks–SMART Money Moves: Setting Financial Goals That Work
description: Stop wishing and start achieving. This workshop introduces you to the widely recognized SMART goals framework (specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound) and shows you how to apply it directly to your finances. Learn to transform vague aspirations into clear, actionable financial plans for saving, spending, and investing.

name: Keep Calm and Carry Yarn
description: Yarn crafters and yarn-craft-curious folks are invited to join a Keep Calm and Carry Yarn gathering in the Lied Library lobby. We invite thread and embroidery floss crafters to join us! Bring your current work in progress, or stop by for inspiration and conversation. The Libraries' Makerspace will provide material samples and other supplies. Note: Completing a makerspace orientation is not required for these events.

name: Mind the Gap: Using Sources to Present New Ideas
description: A good research question can’t be answered by consulting just one source! Whether you’re filling a research gap by asking a completely new question or looking at a topic from a new angle, the research process involves grounding your perspective in the context of previous knowledge using a number of different sources. In this interactive workshop, we will explore how to use a combination of sources to support your research claims.

name: Calligraphy Workshop
