You will learn how to use traditional inkstones and how to maintain your brushes properly. Through guided demonstrations and individual practice, students will develop confidence and control over their brushwork. By the end of the workshop, students will be able to create their own finished calligraphy piece.

This year’s winners have demonstrated extraordinary musicianship and technical mastery, and their performances promise to deliver a captivating musical experience.

Ensuring Integrity: Cybersecurity and AI in the Gambling Sector
description: Cybersecurity breaches in the gambling industry can have wide-reaching and costly consequences. Attacks can lead to gambling platforms being shut down, hackers gaining player account data, and unauthorized access to payment systems, leading to direct revenue loss and regulatory consequences. Cybersecurity is regulated at both the federal and state levels. When responding to incidents, stakeholders must walk a fine line between fairness, integrity, and player safety.

location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Art Exhibition: Moth Ball
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Window Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Moth Ball, an installation of a single risograph print in the Museum’s Window Gallery.

name: Graduate College: How to Use Social Media for Professionals
description: How can you make the most of social media for your professional growth and networking? This workshop is tailored for graduate and professional students aiming to harness social media to build personal brands, connect with industry leaders, and advance their careers. Learn preferred practices for creating and maintaining a professional online presence, strategies for engaging with your audience, and tips for leveraging platforms like LinkedIn.

name: Dissertation Defense: Richard "Rich" Chang
description: Richard Chang, Ph.D. Candidate Department of Psychology Voices Under Constraint: College Students’ Self-Censorship, Empowered Resistance, And Solidarity Advisory Committee Members Gloria Wong-Padoongpatt, Advisory Committee Chair Rachael Robnett, Committee Member Paul Nelson, Committee Member Christine Clark, Graduate College Representative

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Virtual: Master of Arts - Urban Leadership Information Session
description: Please join us to review the Master of Arts Urban Leadership! Aimed at actively engaged working professionals, this degree is offered online to maximize student flexibility, with optional in-person experiential opportunities available.

name: FYE- Rebels Rising Up Day: Milestone Preview
description: Join First-Year Experience to get a preview of learning more about our UNLV Resources and Rebels Rising Up Day as you rise to your second-year at UNLV!

name: Snack 'n Study: Get Stuff Done!
description: Join us at the Lied Library for a chill and productive study session. Study individually or with a group. Coffee, tea, and snacks will be provided.

name: Beyond the Deficit: Relationships and Rigor: Institutional and Classroom Contexts: Using Data & Tools to Identify Equity Gaps
description: Institutional and Classroom Contexts: Using Data & Tools to Identify Equity Gaps introduces participants to ways they can leverage a range of tools and data to better serve UNLV students.

name: Snacks and Skills: Digital Advocacy
description: Join us for an empowering Powerpuff Girls-themed Snacks and Skills. Explore how social media and online platforms can drive powerful change in this interactive session on digital advocacy. Rooted in the Social Change Wheel 2.0, we'll discuss ethical engagement, digital storytelling, and mobilizing communities online. Learn how to amplify your voice and causes you care about in today’s digital age. Build your skills while you snack and scroll for justice.

name: Global Thursday: Culture and Conversation
description: Learn about different cultures and share your own, enjoy international snacks, and make new friends every Global Thursday. All UNLV students are welcome!

name: 3D Printing Workshop
description: If you are new to 3D printing this workshop is for you! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. Please be aware that this workshop leads you through the process of 3D printing and time will not be allocated towards your specific project.

name: Campus Conversations for Peace and Civility
description: Join fellow faculty and staff for an interactive virtual session dedicated to strengthening our campus culture. In this online workshop, we will explore practical strategies to cultivate dialogue rooted in peace, mutual respect, and constructive engagement. Moderator José Meléndrez, AVP, Academic Partnerships & Initiatives Opening Remarks Kate Korgan, Ph.D., Acting Executive Vice President & Provost Guest Speakers:

name: University Forum Lecture: The Fate of the Americas: The Cuban Missile Crisis and the Cold War
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: Renata Keller, associate professor of history at University of Nevada, Reno, will discuss the deadly riots that shook Bolivia when news of the Cuban Missile Crisis broke, the naval quarantine that members of Argentina's armed forces formed around Cuba, the pro-Castro demonstrations organized by Nicaraguan students, and more. This lecture demonstrates that even at the brink of destruction, Latin Americans played active roles in global politics and inter-American relations. Co-sponsored by Tom Wright, Distinguished Emeritus Professor of History, UNLV.

name: Intro To Triathlon - Cycling
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Fitness Studio 1110
description: Join us in preparation for the Triathlon Spring event on April 4. During this class we will guide you through different exercises, techniques and strategies for preparing for the cycling leg of a triathlon. We invite anyone regardless of cycling level or triathlon experience to join us in this free event.

name: Daniel Cox Performs a Doctor of Musical Arts Tuba Recital
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Enjoy a performance of 19th- and 20th-century works by Schumann, Koetsier, Plog, and Lundquist. Parking

location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Percussion Studio, Room 235 (Second Floor)
description: Austin Russell’s Junior recital will feature Alexander Felix on percussion. This recital will feature works by Robert Aldridge, Korry Friend, Nicholas Hall, and Brian Mason.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
UNLVolunteers - Community in Action: The Just One Project
location:
Student Union
        Eating Bar - Under 1st floor stairs
description: Meet us on Friday, March 27 at the Student Union Eating Bar at 8 a.m. for breakfast and conversation before we head out to volunteer with The Just One Project. The Just One Project is a local nonprofit committed to fighting food insecurity and increasing access to food, resources, and essential services through their Food For All and Community Connect programs. Volunteers will assist with assembling grocery bags and boxes of canned and soft goods that will be used for senior home deliveries. Volunteers may also help unload pallets of food and restock warehouse shelves.

name: Rebel Red Friday
description: Non-local students and families who are searching for a college are invited to join us for an exclusive day at UNLV! Immerse yourself in our campus life, hear directly from current students, explore our diverse colleges, and discover our housing and dining options. You'll even have the opportunity to fill out an application on the spot.

name: 2026 Neuroscience Research Showcase
description: Nexus-Neuro: Integrating Science, Medicine, and Society You are invited to participate in the 2026 Spring Neuroscience Research Showcase, a campus-wide symposium highlighting ongoing research in addictions, aging and health disparities, and neuroscience. This event serves as a hub to spark new collaborations across our growing interdisciplinary research communities. The day will feature engaging oral and poster presentations, student presentation awards, and dedicated time for networking.

name: Art Exhibition: My Culture’s Disease Has Got ...
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Donna Beam Gallery (Room 145)
description: The Donna Beam Gallery presents the MFA Thesis Exhibition of Dan45 Hernandez, My Culture’s Disease Has Got the Best of Me. Hernandez is an interdisciplinary artist from Las Vegas, Nevada who holds a BFA in Sculpture from UNLV and is currently pursuing his MFA.

name: Capture the Flag Competition with C2Society
location:
Thomas T. Beam Engineering Complex - Building A
        Great Hall
description: Join the College of Engineering and the Lee Business School for our spring 2026 Capture the Flag Competition (CTF). This CTF will cover topics including cryptography, forensics, log analysis, OSINT, reverse engineering and more. Prizes for the top scoring team will be given out, so come and give it your best shot! New to CTFs? Don’t worry, all levels are welcome and C2Society will have mentors on hand to assist anyone who may need help.

name: Courageous Dialogue Workshop
description: This event will be a two-hour, in-person program to help participants practice the skills and principles of engaging in meaningful conversation that is open, authentic, and respectful. This event is open to all UNLV students with a current student ID. Students planning to attend should RSVP here on the Involvement Center.

name: Mindfulness Brief Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Eastern Standard.

name: Guided Imagery Practice
description: The mind is a powerful tool! Join us in this brief relaxation exercise as we focus our minds, using our imagination. No experience is necessary. Participants will be briefly educated on what guided imagery is then led through a series of verbal cues and suggestions into their own private place of rest and restoration. Approximately 15 minutes total.

name: Publishing Matters: Digital Accessibility - What Does It Mean for Editors?
description: How do digital accessibility requirements and recommendations change the work of journal editors? Two experts in the scholarly publishing community will discuss ways that journal editors can utilize digital accessibility and expand the potential readership of their publications. Guest Speakers Stacy Scott, Accessibility Officer at Taylor & Francis Lauren Stachew, Senior Digital Publishing Coordinator at University of Michigan Library

name: National AI Literacy Day Event at UNLV
description: On March 27, UNLV will recognize National AI Literacy Day with a practical, campus-wide event focused on strengthening AI literacy in teaching, learning, research, and administrative work. This 90-minute virtual session will introduce the current landscape of AI at UNLV and highlight how different areas of campus are engaging with AI literacy. Through a keynote and a series of lightning talks from colleagues across the university, participants will learn where AI is already showing up in campus practice and where to find resources and support.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Department of Economics Seminar Series: Hale Utar, Grinnell College
description: The Department of Economics would like to invite you to a seminar by Professor Hale Utar from the Grinnell College Utar’s research interests are in international trade and macroeconomics, with a focus on how trade and the macroeconomic environment affect firms and workers.

name: 16th Annual Internship & Career Fair
location:
Paul B. Sogg Architecture Building
        Architecture Studies Library
description: This event is free and open to all, including students, alumni, faculty, staff, and the community. The focused fair invites students from all disciplines of the School of Architecture together with representatives from several professional design firms across the AEC industry interested in recruiting summer interns from all year levels or full-time employees from our graduating or graduated classes.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: "Conversion to Judaism: A Model for Ethnoracial Identity Change?"
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room 122
description: Amy Reed-Sandoval, Dept. of Philosophy, UNLV—¡Mami sí es mexicana!, my then-four-year-old daughter shouted, tears in her eyes. I was trying to explain that while she and her father are Mexican, I am not. She seemed stunned by my words. Why would I deny all the ways in which Mexican culture is part of our familial life, from the language we speak at home, to the foods we eat, to our relationship to Mexico itself?

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: Interactive Forum: Life After College
description: Join us for an interactive session engaging in meaningful conversations about navigating life after college, exploring career paths, and transitioning into the professional world!

name: BMI Live: Nature is What We Come To in the Practice of Remembering featuring Saretta Morgan with Lazarus Letcher, Erica Vital-Lazare, and Claytee D. White
location:
New West Las Vegas Library
1861 N. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd. Las Vegas, NV 89106
description: This conversation opens a space to celebrate the rich perspectives of Black artists, social change makers, and everyday folks who’ve deepened our capacities to feel, observe, and be present to natural worlds that are often weaponized against us as Black people shaped in various ways by the U.S. South (east/west).

name: Tesura Quartet Chamber Music Concert
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: The Tesura Quartet will perform string quartets by Haydn, Shostakovitch, and Tchaikovsky. Comprised of violinists Sophia Perez-Monroe and Aaron Tseng, violist John Lee, and cellist Matthew Lee, the ensemble was formed in 2024 and has established a reputation as one of the most exciting quartets in the Midwest. Based in Kansas City, the quartet formed out of a passion for music. Since then, the quartet has been selected to participate in festivals, competitions, and masterclasses hosted by some of the country’s leading musicians.

name: Nevada Conservatory Theatre & A Public Fit: Passage
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Black Box Theatre
description: by Christopher Chen a co-production with A Public Fit Theatre Company directed by Ann-Marie Pereth and Joseph D. Kucan

location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
2026 Corporate Challenge: Esports (A)
location:
Becker Family Tech
2221 Maverick St
Las Vegas, NV 89108
description: Step into the digital arena for a high-energy competition that blends strategy, teamwork, and fast decision-making. The Esports Challenge brings colleagues together to compete in organized video game matchups designed to test communication, coordination, and quick thinking under pressure.

name: West Coast Mariachi Group & Vocal Competitions
description: Feel the excitement as talented young artists from across the country compete fiercely for top honors, prizes, and a coveted spot in the prestigious Mariachi Extravaganza concert. From brilliant musicianship to powerful, heartfelt vocals, each performance will highlight the beauty, emotion, and vibrant spirit of the mariachi music tradition.

name: Graduate College: Grad Rebel Writing Boot Camp - Weekend Edition
location:
University Gateway Building
        Gather Rooms 268 and 269
description: Designed for students with weekday commitments, the Grad Rebel Writing Boot Camp: Weekend Edition offers focused support to help you build stronger writing skills and make real progress on your writing projects. This in-person program includes hands-on activities, workshops, and dedicated time for structured writing—all in a supportive, distraction-free environment. The weekend boot camp is hosted at the Graduate College, with breakfast and lunch provided each day.

name: Open Mic with Jean Nidetch Care Center and Spotlight Poetry
location: Velvet Vine Wine (511 S Las Vegas Blvd)
description: Your voice belongs in the spotlight! The Jean Nidetch Care Center is proud to sponsor an open mic with Spotlight Poetry as part of the Phenomenal Womxn Poetry Festival. Bring your poetry, spoken word, or personal story to the stage. This open mic centers resilience, healing, and the power of using one’s voice.

location:
4734 Horse Dr., 
North Las Vegas, NV 89084
On-campus carpool pickup: SU First Floor Eating Bar
description: Join UNLVolunteers with our semesterly event with the Center for Urban Water Conservation. We will help weed the area and learn about native plants and water conservation. Please RSVP on the Involvement Center.

name: Sole Hope Shoe Party
location:
Lied Library
        Graduate Student Commons, Room 2141
description: Sole Hope is a nonprofit organization dedicated to combating foot diseases in East Africa, particularly through the prevention of jigger infestations. We will focus on educating participants about jiggers, supporting communities through medical aid, and contributing to job creation in Uganda. Participants will use donated blue jeans to cut out shoe patterns, which will be sent to Uganda for final assembly by local shoemakers.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Study, Sip & Socialize with Comadres
description: Join us for our monthly writing session created to support your scholarship, your goals, and your community. Whether you’re working on seminar papers, comprehensive exams, articles, your thesis, or dissertation, this is a dedicated space to write with intention alongside fellow comadres. Build momentum, share encouragement, and connect while navigating graduate school together. Come for the focus, stay for the comunidad.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Table Tennis (A)
location:
Dula Gym
451 E Bonanza Rd
Las Vegas, NV 89101
description: Fast. competitive, and full of energy. the Table Tennis Challenge brings quick reflexes and sharp strategy to the spotlight. Participants will face off in singles or double matches, rallying back and forth in a game that rewards precision, control, and anticipation.

name: An Evening of Masterful Music Featuring Brahms, Gustav Mahler, and Alma Mahler
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Finn Caoile's graduate recital brings together three works from the German Romantic era, united by the artistic relationships of Johannes Brahms and the Mahlers. The program opens with Brahms's Trio in A Minor for Clarinet, Cello, and Piano, opus 114, performed with Chris Armeno on clarinet and Christian Garcia on cello. This is followed by Gustav Mahler's Kindertotenlieder song cycle, performed with Jawan Jenkins, baritone, and concludes with Fünf Lieder by Alma Mahler, performed with Elisabeth Rose Leone, mezzo-soprano.

name: Jonathan Palomar, Mariachi Revolución, & Competition Winners
description: With national mariachi group and vocal competition winners and featured guest artist Jonathan Palomar Immerse yourself in an unforgettable evening of music, culture, and tradition at the 2nd Annual Mariachi Extravaganza—Las Vegas. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: The Good, The Bad and The Ugly: Social Media, AI, and Mental Health
description: “Who am I?”, “Why don’t I have what they have”, and “Is something wrong with me”? Many of us have had these questions, probably more than once. We often turn to social media or AI for the answers, but that can come with both costs and benefits. Join us to learn more about the potential impacts of social media and AI use on your mental health.

name: Rethinking the Hierarchy of Evidence
description: Whose work gets recognized as “real” research, and whose is dismissed? Why do some methods get held up as rigorous while others are seen as less valid? In this session, we’ll look at how traditional evidence hierarchies came to be, the limits they create, and how they shape whose voices are included in scholarship. Together, we’ll explore other ways of thinking about rigor and how broadening our view of evidence can open space for more representative knowledge production.

name: Writing Essentials Wednesdays
description: This semester, join the Writing Center on the first Wednesday of each month at 1 p.m. to strengthen your writing habits and skills. This spring, we'll be focusing on starting out strong. Have a paper due soon that you haven't yet begun? Were you just assigned an essay for class? Attend this workshop to learn strategies to set yourself up for success. We'll touch on topics such as turning your assignment prompt into a helpful checklist; organizing your ideas; and citing your sources.

name: Speakeasy Series: Speak Your Success
description: Share your successes, wins, and celebrate! Celebrate with sparkling cider and faux champagne drinks. Join us for a drop-in space to eat and share your story. Meet with other students and CAPS staff to speak your story, give and get support, and engage in activities for reflection and growth.

name: Rethinking the Hierarchy of Evidence
description: Whose work gets recognized as “real” research, and whose is dismissed? Why do some methods get held up as rigorous while others are seen as less valid? In this session, we’ll look at how traditional evidence hierarchies came to be, the limits they create, and how they shape whose voices are included in scholarship. Together, we’ll explore other ways of thinking about rigor and how broadening our view of evidence can open space for more representative knowledge production.

name: "Non-Compact Proofs"
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room 122
description: Sergei Artemov, Dept. of Philosophy, The Graduate Center, CUNY— Non-compact proofs are used in mathematics but overlooked in the analysis of (un)provability of consistency. We focus on arithmetical proofs of universal statements (*) "for any natural number n, F(n)." A proof of (*) is compact if all proofs of F(n)'s for n=0,1,2,... fit into some finitely axiomatized fragment of Peano Arithmetic PA. An example of non-compact reasoning is the standard proof of Mostowski's 1952 reflexivity theorem: PA proves the consistency of its finite fragments.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Valley of Fire: Hiking Through Nature's Colorful Canvas
description: Come explore the almost alien-looking landscape of Valley of Fire State Park, right here in Nevada! Known for its vibrant rock formations and unique geological features, this park offers some of the most striking scenery in the U.S.—and on this trip, you’ll get to hike through it. Join us as we discover the incredible geology of this Nevada treasure. The trip includes transportation, equipment, instruction, and food.

name: Rebel Rec Sprint Triathlon
description: Join Rebel Rec for our Annual Reverse Triathlon—a fun and inclusive event designed for all fitness levels! Whether you're a seasoned athlete or trying a triathlon for the first time, this race offers a supportive and exciting way to challenge yourself. Participants can compete individually or as part of a team, with several race options available to match your goals and experience level. Choose from competitive and non-competitive divisions, team relay, biathlon (with hand cranks available instead of the spin bike), or a 5K-only option.

location:
Student Union

description: Join UNLVolunteers at Gilcrease Nature Sanctuary on April 4! Our group will be cleaning up the goat enclosures (and maybe giving goats some scratches) and light landscaping like raking up yard debris like leaves. Please arrive at this address by 9 a.m.: 8103 Racel Street Las Vegas, NV 89131. We will provide transportation, if you require. Please indicate so when you RSVP and arrive at the SU eating bar by 8 a.m. for carpooling. We will also provide food afterwards (at noon), and be arriving back at UNLV by 1:30 p.m.

description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during this time. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

Gratitude Brief Practice

name: It's Just Coffee: Athletics at University
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups (approximately once a month) for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning.

location:

name: Traditional Chinese Medicine Week Day 1
location:
Student Union
        Lecture Location: SU Room 211
Exercise and Activities: Pida Plaza
description: Join us for day 1 of a two-day cultural and wellness experience introducing the philosophy and practices of traditional Chinese medicine. This day will feature a foundational lecture followed by interactive, beginner-friendly movement sessions and hands-on experiences held outdoors in Pida Plaza. Participants will have the opportunity to explore traditional Chinese wellness practices, including:

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Yoga Fitness Class
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        SRWC Meeting Room 1010
description: Take a mindful mid-day break with our 30-minute lunch time yoga series, designed specifically for UNLV faculty and staff. These sessions offer a refreshing blend of gentle movement, stretching, and breathwork to help you reset, reduce stress, and return to your day with renewed focus and energy. No experience is necessary—just bring a yoga mat, wear comfortable clothing, and come as you are. ﻿ ﻿Step away from your desk and join us for a well-deserved pause. Your mind and body will thank you!

name: Research Impact Through NSF I-Corps

name: Student Veteran and First Generation Study Night in the Library
description: Calling all student veterans and first-generation students! Need help with coursework or looking for a supportive study space? Join us for student veterans and first-generation study night in the library. Drop-in any time during the event to connect with fellow student veterans and first-gen students, access libraries resources, and receive academic support from the libraries. Sponsored by: UNLV Libraries, Rebel Vets, and UNLV First Gen Club.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        CHB C311

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Indoor Volleyball (A)
location:
Dula Gym
451 E Bonanza Rd
Las Vegas, NV 89101
description: Step onto the court for the high-energy, team-focused competition that combines skill, communication, and quick reflexes. In the indoor volleyball Challenge, teams work together to serve, set, and spike their way past opponents, aiming to score points while keeping the ball in play.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Meet with a TIAA Representative on Campus
description: Meet with a TIAA financial consultant to help create a financial plan for your future. Discuss investments, saving and withdrawing from your plan when you retire. All employees are welcome to meet with a consultant.

name: Spring 2026 Grad Fest
description: Get ready to celebrate your milestone in true Rebel fashion! Join us for Grad Fest 2026 to explore everything you need for commencement – from caps and gowns to announcements, class rings, and more. It is your one-stop shop to prepare for graduation, connect with campus partners, and celebrate with fellow grads.

name: Medical District Blood Drive
description: Join Us for our first annual medical district blood drive! This is a special opportunity for students, faculty, and staff to come together and make a meaningful impact in our community. Every donation has the power to save lives. Not able to donate? You can still be part of the effort by sharing the event with classmates and colleagues. Every bit of support helps. Let’s come together as a Medical District community and start a new tradition of giving back. We hope to see you there!

name: Journalism & Media Studies Internship and Career Fair
description: Join the Hank Greenspun School of Journalism and Media studies on Wed. April 8 from 10 a.m. - 1 p.m. in the main lobby of Greenspun Hall for the School's annual Internship and Career Fair.

name: Tools for Creating and Delivering Presentations in Online Classes
description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. This session helps students create clear, engaging, and accessible presentations for online classes using Google Slides or Canva. Students will learn when to use each tool and how to design slides that communicate ideas effectively.

name: On Campus Employer Visit: Target Hospitality - Day 1
location:
Hospitality Hall
        Hospitality Hall Grand Lobby
description: If you are an upcoming graduate exploring post-graduation opportunities, we encourage you to connect with Target Hospitality during their upcoming campus visit. Target Hospitality will be on-site to promote their assistant manager intern position, a unique pathway into management that differs from traditional hotel training programs. We strongly encourage graduating seniors to stop by the table, ask questions, and learn more about this opportunity.

name: Botanical Prints: A Gelli Printmaking Workshop
description: Celebrate spring in Lied Library's Makerspace as we create foraged art! We will take a walk into the nature of campus and forage simple leaves or flowers, press them onto a Gelli plate, and make our very own botanical prints. All materials for printmaking will be provided! We will be doing a bit of walking so it is recommended that you bring comfortable shoes and a bottle of water.

name: Beginners' Idea Bridge Workshop Series: Viability of a Trademark
description: This workshop will teach you about confusingly similar trademarks, the strength of trademarks, and how to search for trademarks in the USPTOs database. You will participate in learning activities to increase your understanding of trademark topics.

name: Narcan Training
description: Join us for a Narcan Training focused on opioid overdose awareness and response. Participants will learn what opioids are, how to recognize the signs of an overdose, and the steps to safely intervene and help save a life if ever needed. This training centers on harm reduction, community care, and building confidence to respond in an emergency. Attendees will receive free Narcan, a CPR mask, and a first aid kit to take home. Chick-fil-A will be provided while supplies last on a first-come, first-served basis.

name: Success Series: Money Matters #2
description: This workshop will cover essential financial topics, including key financial terms, saving money, financing your education, setting financial goals, budgeting, making smart financial decisions after graduation, investing, and planning for major purchases. Equip yourself with the knowledge and skills to confidently navigate your financial journey from student life to your future career. For more information, visit the Student Success Center Coaching website.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Satisfactory Academic (SAP) Workshop
description: Feeling a little confused about Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) and what it means for your financial aid? No worries, you're not alone! Come to "From Criteria to Clarity: Navigating SAP Standards and the Appeal Process" webinar. We'll break down the SAP policy for keeping your financial aid in simple terms. Plus, we'll show you exactly what to do if you ever need to appeal. Get the info you need to stay on track and keep your aid flowing!

name: Realign For Palestine: Pragmatic Palestinian Perspective On The Future Of Gaza, Israel, & The Middle East
description: On Wednesday, April 8, UNLV will host the internationally renowned Palestinian-American humanitarian, peace activist, and “radical pragmatist,” Ahmed Fouad Alkhatib. Ahmed is a resident senior fellow in Middle East security studies at the Atlantic Council. He is the founder and leader of “Realign for Palestine,” an intellectual and political movement to challenge entrenched narratives around Israel and Palestine and to advance a new policy framework for pro-Palestine advocacy.

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Relaxing Your Mind: Coping With Anxiety
description: This two-part workshop is intended to provide information about anxiety and a variety of practical coping skills for anxiety and stress.

name: Lied Playback Lounge
description: Turn up the volume on student creativity. From beats to short films to digital storytelling, explore bold work created in Lied Library’s Multimedia Studios. Come vibe with us for snacks, a live vinyl DJ, and libraries swag.

name: Palaspas and Pasalubong
description: Celebrate Filipino creativity with hands-on crafts inspired by palaspas, the art of weaving palm leaves into meaningful designs. Get crafty, learn about the tradition, and snack your way through a selection of Filipino treats and candy. You’ll leave with a handmade creation and a sweet pasalubong to remember the fun.

name: Recovery Curious
location:
Student Services Complex - Building A
        CAPS Suite 143
description: Are you curious about substance use recovery or ways to reduce use? Join us virtually or in-person to share questions or receive support around the topic of substance use and recovery.

name: RSI Workshop: Feedback Feedback – Using Multiple Methods to Provide Meaningful Feedback
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Feedback. This training will focus on the following outcomes: Explore learning theories behind multimedia learning and its efficacy Identify WebCampus multimedia tools for optimal student engagement Identify appropriate places in your course to utilize multimedia

name: Traditional Chinese Medicine Week Day 2
description: Join us for day 2 of a two-day cultural and wellness experience exploring the philosophy and practices of traditional Chinese medicine. This day features guided movement sessions, interactive wellness demonstrations, and a special closing lecture, offering participants a deeper look into traditional healing practices. Participants will have the opportunity to experience: Baduanjin (Eight-Section Brocade) Tai Chi Traditional Chinese medicine therapy demonstrations

name: Community in Action: Farm Friends
description: Come out on volunteer with the All Friends Animal Sanctuary! A non-profit sanctuary for abused, homeless, and neglected farm animals in Las Vegas, committed to rescuing, rehabilitating, and caring for these animals while also educating the community on compassionate and sustainable living. We will be meal prepping for the animals and cleaning resident areas as well as scrubbing water pools! Please bring a water flask/bottle; wear clothes you don't mind getting dirty/dusty; and wear close-toed shoes!

name: 2026 State of the University Address
location:
Thomas and Mack Center
        Strip View Pavillion
description: Join UNLV Interim President Chris Heavey for the annual State of the University Address. This signature event brings together students, faculty, staff, alumni, and community partners to reflect on UNLV’s progress, celebrate accomplishments, and look ahead to upcoming priorities and opportunities. All are welcome and encouraged to attend. The address will also be livestreamed on YouTube

name: Calligraphy Workshop
description: In this six-week workshop, students will be introduced to all the essential materials, techniques, and foundational skills needed for Chinese calligraphy. You will learn how to use traditional inkstones and how to maintain your brushes properly. Through guided demonstrations and individual practice, students will develop confidence and control over their brushwork. By the end of the workshop, students will be able to create their own finished calligraphy piece.

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Hula Hālāwai
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Rooms 1020 and 2025
description: Join us for an interactive experience centered on hula as both movement and storytelling. What to Expect Guided introduction to hula Exploration of rhythm, tradition, and meaning A welcoming, all-levels environment Come ready to move, learn, and connect through cultural practice. This event is held in partnership with Rebel Rec.

name: Hula & Halawai
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 2025 and 1010
description: Join us for a Hula dance class and some great snacks! Hula class will be from 5 - 6 p.m. Education portion and snack session will be from 6 - 7 p.m.

name: Network & Create: Grad Rebel Appreciation Zine Mixer
location:
University Gateway Building
        2nd Floor, Gather Conference Room
description: In celebration of Graduate-Professional Student Appreciation Week, join us on Wednesday, April 8, 2026, from 5:30 - 7 p.m. in the University Gateway Building (GTW) Gather Conference Room for a creative and relaxed evening. This mixer is the perfect opportunity for graduate and professional students to come together, meet new faces, and showcase their creativity by getting hands-on with supplies to create their very own zine.

name: Learn to Kayak
description: Learn to kayak in the controlled environment of the SRWC pool! Our experienced trip leaders will guide you through a two-hour course on how to properly use a kayak and get you ready for the open water.

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: College of Hospitality - April Alumni Mixer
location:
Ole Red Las Vegas
3627 South Las Vegas Boulevard
Las Vegas, NV 89109
description: Calling all hospitality alumni! Get ready to mix, mingle, and reconnect at the Hospitality Alumni Monthly Mixer. Join us on Wednesday, April 8, at Ole Red Las Vegas for our April Alumni Mixer. Reconnect with fellow Rebels in a fun, high-energy setting right on the Strip. Our friends at Ole Red will be providing light appetizers and one drink ticket per registered guest (wine, beer, or non-alcoholic beverage). Enjoy great food, great company, and meaningful connections within our Hospitality alumni community.

name: Tools for Creating and Delivering Presentations in Online Classes
description: Online Learning Labs are guided, hands-on workshops hosted by UNLV Online Student Support that help students build real skills in UNLV’s online tools. This session helps students create clear, engaging, and accessible presentations for online classes using Google Slides or Canva. Students will learn when to use each tool and how to design slides that communicate ideas effectively.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
It's Just Coffee: Athletics at the University
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups - approximately once a month - for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning.

name: Art Exhibition: Moth Ball
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Window Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Moth Ball, an installation of a single risograph print in the Museum’s Window Gallery.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: On Campus Employer Visit: Target Hospitality - Day 2
location:
Hospitality Hall
        Hospitality Hall Grand Lobby
description: If you are an upcoming graduate exploring post-graduation opportunities, we encourage you to connect with Target Hospitality during their upcoming campus visit. Target Hospitality will be on-site to promote their assistant manager intern position, a unique pathway into management that differs from traditional hotel training programs. We strongly encourage graduating seniors to stop by the table, ask questions, and learn more about this opportunity.

name: Graduate College: Grad Student Financial Wellness
description: This workshop is designed to help you build confidence in managing your finances during grad school and beyond. Learn practical strategies for budgeting, managing student loans, saving, and planning for future goals, all tailored to the realities of graduate student life. Presented by John Hirano, director of the Institute of Financial Literacy & Wellness.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Art Exhibition: Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Center Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt, an exhibition of memorial cyanotypes by Amy Elkins.

name: Art Exhibition: El camino se traza al andar (The Path is Traced by Walking)
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art West Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking), an exhibition of artworks by the Los Angeles-based interdisciplinary artist Jackie Amézquita.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Lunch and Learn – The Student Survival Guide: How to Own Your College Budget
location:
Student Services Complex - Building C
        Institute for Financial Literacy and Wellness
description: Forget the myth that budgeting is just about saying "no." In reality, a budget is a blueprint for your freedom. Whether you are managing financial aid, a part-time hustle, and your savings for life after the university, owning your cash flow is the ultimate power move. Join us to build a 3-step personalized system that ensures 100% of your money works as hard as you do. Don’t just survive the semester—own it.

name: MGM Resorts Roundtable | Finance Operations
description: Are you curious about how finance drives real-world decision-making in the hospitality industry? Join us for a Rebel Roundtable discussion with Erik Vela, director of finance operations at ARIA/Vdara. Erik will share his career journey and explain how financial and operating reports guide executive-level strategy, performance, and growth.

name: DIY Fidget & Stim Tools Workshop
description: Join the University Libraries for a hands-on library workshop teaching you how to make your own fidget and stim tools tailored to your sensory preferences. Leave with custom creations that can support concentration, relaxation, and everyday wellbeing. All supplies provided.

name: Intro to Pre-Health Workshop
description: Join Pre-Professional Advising for our Intro to Pre-Health workshop. This workshop is for all students interested in careers in medicine (MD/DO), dentistry, physician assistant, veterinary medicine, physical therapy, occupational therapy, pharmacy, and optometry. We will discuss the application process, required coursework, and resources available to help you succeed. This workshop is required for first-year students before making an appointment with a pre-professional advisor.

name: Thesis Defense: Jelena Grave De Peralta
description: Jelena Grave De Peralta, MPH Candidate Department of Environmental and Global Health Evaluation of the Collaboration Between a Local Health District and University During the COVID-19 Pandemic in Southern Nevada Advisory Committee Members Brian Labus, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Jennifer Pharr, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Maxim Gakh, J.D., Advisory Committee Member Ms. Xan Goodman, Graduate College Representative

name: Celebrating Your Latinidad Identities Through Connection and Healing
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 240
description: This workshop will invite you to explore your generational super powers and strengths and identify ways that we can heal within our community.

name: Beyond the Deficit: Relationships and Rigor: Syllabus Audit
description: Syllabus Audit guides faculty in creating syllabi that communicate equity, inclusivity, and high expectations.

name: Supporting Victim-Survivors of Sexual Violence Training for UNLV Staff & Faculty
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Hybrid Session:
- In-person: Health Education Room in the Student Recreation and Wellness Center, located on the 1st Floor
- Zoom Link will be sent via email after RSVP
description: For Sexual Assault Awareness Month (SAAM), the Care Center presents: "Supporting Victim-Survivors of Sexual Violence" for Staff & Faculty. This presentation helps Staff and Faculty identify the impact of trauma on students, define the components of trauma-informed care and healing-centered care, and practice how to respond if students were to disclose power-based violence.

name: Plan Year 2027 Plan Design and Rate Changes
location: RSVP to Receive the Location Details
description: This session is designed to help provide a clear overview of each PEBP health insurance plan offered for the next plan year. We will highlight key differences and review rates, so that you are able to select the option that best fits your family’s needs during open enrollment.

name: Non-Tenure Track Group: Should I Become an Undergrad Coordinator?
description: This month's topic: Should I become undergraduate coordinator? A panel of non-tenure track (NTT) faculty will discuss the pros and cons of being their department's undergraduate coordinator. Please join us for one of the two online sessions to learn about being an undergraduate coordinator.

name: Global Thursday: Culture and Conversation
description: Learn about different cultures and share your own, enjoy international snacks, and make new friends every Global Thursday. All UNLV students are welcome!

name: 3D Printing Workshop
description: If you are new to 3D printing this workshop is for you! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. Please be aware that this workshop leads you through the process of 3D printing and time will not be allocated towards your specific project.

name: Technology Entrepreneurship Evening — Unlock the Potential (TEE-UP)
description: Join us for a new monthly evening event series connecting UNLV students with successful technology entrepreneurs! The April TEE-UP event will feature keynote speaker Piotr Tomasik, '09 BS Computer Science, co-founder and president of Tensorwave. What to Expect:

name: University Forum Lecture: Staying Decent In A Criminal State: Berlin Under The Nazis And Other Places Since
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: Ian Buruma will talk about life in Berlin during World War II and how the Nazi regime tried to keep up the pretense that life was normal. The theme is one that will resonate in our own time: how to remain decent in an indecent society. Why did some people refuse to look away from the gross injustices, and even resist while others (the majority) chose to keep their heads down? Co-sponsored by the Departments of History, World Languages and Cultures, and Political Science to the bottom.

name: Caritas: Love in Song - A Master of Music Choral Conducting Recital
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: We invite you to join Jeremy Valdez as he completes his master of music degree with his culminating conducting recital. Caritas: Love in Song — This recital, presented in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the master of music degree, explores the many expressions of love found in both human relationships and spiritual devotion. Featuring music by Eric Whitacre, Peter Cornelius, Healey Willan, and Dan Forrest, the program aims to take the audience on a journey from intimate expressions of personal affection to reflections on enduring spiritual love.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Posture Reset Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Pacific Standard.

name: Meet with a TIAA Representative on Campus
description: Meet with a TIAA financial consultant to help create a financial plan for your future. Discuss investments, saving and withdrawing from your plan when you retire. All employees are welcome to meet with a consultant.

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: Serve & Lead Mini-Con 2026: Taking Flight
description: The Serve & Lead MiniCon is a one-day conference designed to help UNLV students connect their community engagement and leadership development experiences with the careers and callings that await them.

name: UNLVolunteers Breathe in with Obodo
description: Join UNLVolunteers for an afternoon of gardening with Obodo Collective. Take a breath before the stress of the last stretch of the semester. Spend the morning gardening and wind down with a grounding exercise and refreshments. The following items are suggested to ensure a fun, safe, and comfortable experience: Close toe shoes Head covering and sunglasses A light long sleeve shirt or arm sleeves Light, durable pants, loose fit pants

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery

Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: New Faculty Academy: Managing Stress and Rejection
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 240
description: Academic faculty members who are new to UNLV, as well as those who want an inspirational boost, are invited to a panel discussion: Managing Stress and Rejection. This panel addresses the realities of stress and rejection in academic life and offers strategies for navigating them with resilience. Panelists will share personal experiences and practical techniques for maintaining well-being, reframing setbacks, and developing healthy coping mechanisms that allow new faculty to thrive in a demanding environment.

This event is specially designed for our graduate and professional students to enjoy culinary delights, offering a unique opportunity to savor an array of specially tailored dishes at a reduced rate. It's not only a chance to enjoy a delicious meal but also a moment to engage with the co

description: This month's topic: Should I become undergraduate coordinator? A panel of non-tenure track (NTT) faculty will discuss the pros and cons of being their department's undergraduate coordinator. Please join us for one of the two online sessions to learn about being an undergraduate coordinator.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: Know Your Rights : Collaboration with ACLU
description: The African Student Alliance is excited to invite you to our Interactive Forum 2.0 titled “Know Your Rights,” featuring representatives from the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU). This session is designed to empower students with essential knowledge about their rights, provide a space to ask questions, engage in meaningful dialogue, and gain practical insights you can use in everyday situations.

name: Racial Equity & Social Justice Research in the Age of Retrenchment
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building A
        Room A110
description: Join the

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
Yankee Doodle Canyon Expedition
description: Ever wanted to descend into a canyon and explore it from within? This canyoneering expedition to Yankee Doodle Canyon in Utah offers exactly that! This beginner-friendly slot canyon is perfect for anyone seeking the thrill of adventure—no prior experience required. This trip also includes a mandatory 2-hour learning session on April 9 at the climbing wall, where participants will be taught all the essential knots and techniques needed for the canyon descent. The trip includes transportation, equipment, instruction, and food.

description: We’re celebrating the first anniversary of Filipino Town Las Vegas! While the community event is scheduled for 10 a.m. - 8 p.m., members of APIME-FSA are invited to meet up and connect between 10 a.m. - 1 p.m. to enjoy the parade, live performances, and food.

location:
Leavitt Family Park
2100 E St Louis Ave
Las Vegas, NV 89104
description: Bring precision. strategy, and a touch of friendly rivalry to the lawn with the Bocce Challenge! Take turns rolling bocce balls, aiming to land closest to the target ball while strategically blocking opponents and planning their next moves.

name: Art Workshop: Slow Art Day 2026
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Galleries
description: Slow Art Day is a worldwide movement that takes place annually in museums, galleries, artist studios, and any other venue where art can be found. It supports people who want to intensify their experience of the visual world by paying attention to artworks slowly and deeply.

name: Lied Library's Silver Soirée
description: Step into a night of discovery and delight. Lied Library celebrates 25 years at the heart of UNLV with the Silver Soirée, an immersive evening inspired by the magic of learning and the power of possibility. Guests will journey through the library in a progressive experience that reveals collections, innovation, and spaces in unexpected ways, brought to life through inspired cuisine and moments of wonder.

name: Los Angeles Regional Alumni Reception
location:
Perch
448 S. Hill St., Los Angeles, CA 90013
description: The UNLV Alumni Association invites you and a guest to join us at Perch in Los Angeles to enjoy an evening of engaging and informative conversations with Rebels in your area. Hosted beer, wine, and heavy hors d'oeuvres will be available.

2026 Corporate Challenge: Trap and Skeet Shooting (All teams)

Clark County Shooting Complex
11357 N Decatur Blvd
Las Vegas, NV 89131

Alta Ham Fine Arts

name: The Thesis/Dissertation Submission Process: A Guide
location:
Lied Library
        Pioche Room or Online
description: In this lecture-style session, graduate students will get an overview of the submission process for theses and dissertations. We'll also talk about embargoes, accessibility, and how to access usage statistics to leverage in job interviews or further graduate applications.

name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation Practice
description: Come and join us in this practice of relaxation as we slowly work our way across various muscle groups to decrease tension and stress. Progressive Muscle Relaxation or PMR, is a relaxation practice developed in the early 1900’s and has repeatedly proven to help with relaxation of the mind and body. Through increasing awareness of what tension feels like, and then using techniques to help your muscles and body relax, the mind follows. PMR has also been shown to support pain relief, better sleep, and other mind-body benefits.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Rethinking “Marginal” Landscapes: Lifeways and Connections on the Shivwits Plateau
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 212
description: Please join the Department of Anthropology for another weekly Proseminar talk: Title: Rethinking “Marginal” Landscapes: Lifeways and Connections on the Shivwits Plateau Speaker: Karen Harry, Ph.D., Department of Anthropology UNLV

name: Investing is for Everyone
description: Ready to grow your money? Join Chase Bank for a crash course in personal investing! Whether you're just getting started or want to level up your financial game, this session is designed to help you take control of your future and feel confident about investing. Chase experts will break down everything you need to know in a way that actually makes sense — no experience needed. What you'll learn:

name: Worldstrides Visits UNLV
description: The Office of Study Abroad Programs is pleased to welcome Worldstrides to campus on Monday, April 13, 2026. Faculty who are interested in developing or leading international programs are encouraged to attend the sessions taking place in BEH Room 213.

name: 3D Printing Workshop
description: If you are new to 3D printing this workshop is for you! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. Please be aware that this workshop leads you through the process of 3D printing and time will not be allocated towards your specific project.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: UNLV Global Success Series: Relieve Some Stress by Making a Bracelet
description: Relieve some stress by making a bracelet: Enjoy a creative and relaxing way to learn a new skill. If you can line up beads, you can make a bracelet or key chain! Supplies are provided.

name: Sikolohiyang Pilipino: Filipino Psychology
description: Have you ever heard of utang na loob? Walang hiya? Kapwa? Sikolohiyang Pilipino, or Filipino Psychology, has concepts rooted in indigenous practices and values that better capture the Filipino experience. Join us this Asian American History Month in an online workshop to learn more about its history and foundational concepts!

name: Plan Year 2027 Plan Design and Rate Changes
description: This session is designed to help provide a clear overview of each PEBP health insurance plan offered for the next plan year. We will highlight key differences and review rates, so that you are able to select the option that best fits your family’s needs during open enrollment.

name: Virtual: Masters and Doctoral Programs in Music Information Session
description: Learn more about our Masters and Doctoral Programs in Music during this informative session. We will discuss program offerings and how they prepare musicians and music educators for successful careers in performance and education. The School of Music offers enriching graduate degree programs for students pursuing careers in composition, conducting, jazz studies, music education, and performance.

name: Podcasting 101: Basics of Recording & Editing
description: Join David Ramos Candelas, Multimedia and Design Specialist, to learn the basics of podcast recording and editing using technology found inside of Lied Library's Multimedia Production Studios. During this one-hour workshop, you will explore Lied Library's multimedia resources and practice basic recording and editing.

Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: The Archive Awaits: Fanfiction & Finding Your Voice
description: Come meet other students interested in fanfiction! We’ll start with a discussion about how fanfic can build community, foster a sense of belonging, improve your writing skills, and even how fanfic can help you see worlds and characters you love–and maybe even yourself–in new ways. Whether you’re new to fanfic or a seasoned author, this event will feature a variety of activities to facilitate hands-on experience with creating, editing, and publishing your own fic. Refreshments and snacks available!

description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: International Student? Want to Get Hired? Start Here.
description: Want to turn your UNLV experience into real career opportunities both on campus and after graduation? Learn how to build your CV, strengthen your portfolio, and prepare for interviews to land jobs, GAships, and internships. In collaboration with UNLV Career Services & Workforce Development, we are proud to welcome Chris Hughes, career & life design specialist from UNLV Career Services & Workforce Development, sharing expert guidance to help you get career-ready and stand out!

name: Soak Up the Sun: Benefits & Safety + Service Hours
description: Shine bright with the UNLV Wellness Zone and UNLVolunteers at our sun health event! Discover the vital connection between sunlight and your overall health and wellness. Learn about the benefits of Vitamin D, the importance of sun safety, and how to incorporate healthy sun exposure into your routine for improved mood, bone health, and more. Participants will also have the opportunity to create their own sun health kits to take home and give to their community!

name: Virtual: Master of Hospitality Administration Information Session
description: Interested in hospitality administration? Join us to learn more about what the Master of Hospitality Administration at UNLV has to offer!

name: Success Series: Bouncing Back #2
description: The Academic Success Center coaches invite you to attend the "Bouncing Back from Burnout Academic Success" series. Feeling overwhelmed, exhausted, or disconnected? You’re not alone. College life can be demanding, and burnout is something many students face at some point. But the good news is, it’s possible to bounce back and regain your energy, focus, and motivation.

name: Study, Sip & Socialize with Comadres
description: Join us for our monthly writing session created to support your scholarship, your goals, and your community. Whether you’re working on seminar papers, comprehensive exams, articles, your thesis, or dissertation, this is a dedicated space to write with intention alongside fellow comadres. Build momentum, share encouragement, and connect while navigating graduate school together. Come for the focus, stay for the comunidad.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Special Information Session - Application Workshop
description: This Zoom meeting is specifically for those getting ready to apply in the 2026-2027 AMCAS cycle. This is a refresher regarding AMCAS Application highlights and approaches for the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV.

name: Scholarship in Practice Web Lecture Series – Finding a Home: Helping Nevada's Unhoused Students Find Their Way
description: Join us in conversation with leadership and youth from the Nevada Partnership for Homeless Youth (NPHY) to better understand the complexities young people face when experiencing homelessness. Hear directly from youth with lived experience and learn meaningful ways to support and advocate for unhoused young people in our community. Speaker Sophie M. Ladd, Ph.D. – Associate Professor in Residence·University of Nevada, Las Vegas

location:
Lied Library
        Room 2141 Conference Room or Virtual via Zoom
description: Sponsorship Program workshops cover the basics of GPSA Sponsorship funding for graduate and professional student research, travel, project materials, and professional development. There will be time throughout the workshop to ask questions and get specifics on the best techniques for high-quality applications and how to maximize your funding.

name: Beginners' Idea Bridge Workshop Series: Viability of a Patent
description: This workshop will teach you about inventions, parts of a patent, and how to search for patents in the USPTOs database. You will participate in learning activities to increase your understanding of patent topics.
